built with pdoc

utf.thirsty_lang

Thirsty-Lang — A governance-first programming language Part of the Universal Thirsty Family (UTF) language stack.

  1"""
  2Thirsty-Lang — A governance-first programming language
  3Part of the Universal Thirsty Family (UTF) language stack.
  4"""
  5
  6from utf.thirsty_lang.ast import (
  7    ArmorExpr,
  8    AssignStmt,
  9    BinaryOp,
 10    BlockStmt,
 11    BoolLiteral,
 12    CallExpr,
 13    CascadeCall,
 14    ClassDecl,
 15    CleanupStmt,
 16    CombineExpr,
 17    CondenseExpr,
 18    DefendStrat,
 19    DripExpr,
 20    EnumDecl,
 21    ErrorLiteral,
 22    EvaporateExpr,
 23    ExprStmt,
 24    FloatLiteral,
 25    FloodExpr,
 26    ForStmt,
 27    FunctionDecl,
 28    GovernedFunctionDecl,
 29    GuardExpr,
 30    Identifier,
 31    IfStmt,
 32    ImportStmt,
 33    InterfaceDecl,
 34    IntLiteral,
 35    ModuleHeader,
 36    MorphDef,
 37    NewExpr,
 38    NoneLiteral,
 39    PipeExpr,
 40    PipelineExpr,
 41    PourStmt,
 42    Program,
 43    QuenchedLiteral,
 44    ReturnStmt,
 45    SanitizeExpr,
 46    SecurityBlock,
 47    ShadowThirstMutation,
 48    SipStmt,
 49    SpillageStmt,
 50    StringLiteral,
 51    StructDecl,
 52    SymbolExpr,
 53    SymbolStmt,
 54    ThrowStmt,
 55    UnaryOp,
 56    VariableDecl,
 57    WhileStmt,
 58)
 59from utf.thirsty_lang.checker import Checker, check_ast
 60from utf.thirsty_lang.diagnostics import (
 61    ERROR_CODES,
 62    Diagnostic,
 63    DiagnosticBundle,
 64    DiagnosticSeverity,
 65    format_diagnostic,
 66)
 67from utf.thirsty_lang.formatter import format
 68from utf.thirsty_lang.interpreter import Environment, Interpreter
 69from utf.thirsty_lang.lexer import Lexer
 70from utf.thirsty_lang.module_system import (
 71    get_builtin,
 72    list_builtins,
 73    list_stdlib_modules,
 74    resolve_import,
 75)
 76from utf.thirsty_lang.package_manager import PackageManager
 77from utf.thirsty_lang.parser import Parser
 78from utf.thirsty_lang.token import KEYWORDS, Token, TokenType
 79from utf.thirsty_lang.typesys import (
 80    AnyType,
 81    BoolType,
 82    EnumType,
 83    ErrorType,
 84    FloatType,
 85    FunctionType,
 86    GenericType,
 87    GovernedType,
 88    InterfaceType,
 89    IntType,
 90    QuenchedType,
 91    ReservoirType,
 92    ResultType,
 93    StringType,
 94    StructType,
 95    TaskType,
 96    Type,
 97    TypeVariable,
 98    VoidType,
 99    is_assignable,
100    type_to_string,
101    unify,
102)
103
104
105# Lazy import to avoid RuntimeWarning about sys.modules
106def main():
107    from utf.thirsty_lang.cli import main as _cli_main
108    return _cli_main()
109
110
111try:
112    from importlib.metadata import version as _version
113    __version__ = _version("thirsty-lang")
114except Exception:
115    __version__ = "0.8.0"  # fallback
116__all__ = [
117    "Token", "TokenType", "KEYWORDS",
118    "Lexer",
119    "Program", "ModuleHeader",
120    "FunctionDecl", "ClassDecl", "VariableDecl",
121    "BinaryOp", "UnaryOp",
122    "IfStmt", "WhileStmt", "ForStmt",
123    "ReturnStmt", "PourStmt", "SipStmt",
124    "ImportStmt", "AssignStmt", "ExprStmt", "BlockStmt",
125    "IntLiteral", "FloatLiteral", "StringLiteral", "BoolLiteral", "NoneLiteral",
126    "ErrorLiteral", "QuenchedLiteral",
127    "Identifier", "CallExpr",
128    "PipeExpr", "GuardExpr",
129    "SecurityBlock", "SanitizeExpr", "ArmorExpr",
130    "MorphDef", "DefendStrat",
131    "EnumDecl", "StructDecl", "InterfaceDecl",
132    "GovernedFunctionDecl",
133    "SpillageStmt", "CleanupStmt", "ThrowStmt", "CascadeCall",
134    "NewExpr", "FloodExpr", "DripExpr", "EvaporateExpr", "CondenseExpr",
135    "SymbolExpr", "SymbolStmt", "PipelineExpr", "CombineExpr",
136    "ShadowThirstMutation",
137    "Parser",
138    "Type", "IntType", "FloatType", "BoolType", "StringType", "VoidType", "AnyType", "ErrorType",
139    "GenericType", "QuenchedType", "ReservoirType", "TaskType", "ResultType", "GovernedType",
140    "EnumType", "StructType", "InterfaceType",
141    "TypeVariable", "FunctionType",
142    "is_assignable", "type_to_string", "unify",
143    "Checker", "check_ast",
144    "Interpreter", "Environment",
145    "Diagnostic", "DiagnosticBundle", "DiagnosticSeverity", "ERROR_CODES", "format_diagnostic",
146    "resolve_import", "get_builtin", "list_stdlib_modules", "list_builtins",
147    "format",
148    "PackageManager",
149    "main",
150]
@dataclass
class Token: 
227@dataclass
228class Token:
229    type: TokenType
230    lexeme: str
231    line: int = 1
232    col: int = 1
233
234    def __repr__(self):
235        return f"Token({self.type.name}, '{self.lexeme}', L{self.line}:{self.col})"
Token( type: TokenType, lexeme: str, line: int = 1, col: int = 1)
type: TokenType
lexeme: str
line: int = 1
col: int = 1
class TokenType(enum.Enum): 
  6class TokenType(Enum):
  7    # --- Core Keywords ---
  8    DRINK = auto()
  9    POUR = auto()
 10    SIP = auto()
 11    THIRSTY = auto()
 12    HYDRATED = auto()
 13    THIRST = auto()
 14    QUENCH = auto()
 15    REFILL = auto()
 16    TIMES = auto()
 17    GLASS = auto()
 18    RESERVOIR = auto()
 19    WELL = auto()
 20    OF = auto()
 21    FLOOD = auto()
 22    DRIP = auto()
 23    EVAPORATE = auto()
 24    CONDENSE = auto()
 25    FOUNTAIN = auto()
 26    RETURN = auto()
 27    PARCHED = auto()
 28    QUENCHED = auto()
 29    EMPTY = auto()
 30    MUT = auto()
 31    IN = auto()
 32    LET = auto()
 33    FOR = auto()
 34
 35    # --- Module Keywords ---
 36    IMPORT = auto()
 37    FROM = auto()
 38    AS = auto()
 39
 40    # --- Security Keywords ---
 41    # ── Reserved Security Tokens (Tier 5/6 — TSCG / TSCG-B) ──
 42    # These keywords are syntactically parsed but not semantically
 43    # enforced in Thirsty-Lang core (Tier 1). They are reserved for
 44    # use by Shadow Thirst / TSCG governance transformers at higher
 45    # tiers. See tarl/spec.py for T.A.R.L. policy definitions and
 46    # docs/governance_model.md for the tier escalation model.
 47    SHIELD = auto()      # identity/access context barrier
 48    SANITIZE = auto()    # data scrubbing annotation
 49    ARMOR = auto()       # runtime safety wrap
 50    MORPH = auto()       # type coercion boundary
 51    DETECT = auto()      # anomaly tap point
 52    DEFEND = auto()      # invariant enforcement hook
 53
 54    # --- Thirst of Gods Keywords ---
 55    CASCADE = auto()
 56    THIS = auto()
 57    NEW = auto()
 58    PUBLIC = auto()
 59    PRIVATE = auto()
 60    AWAIT = auto()
 61    SPILLAGE = auto()
 62    CLEANUP = auto()
 63    FINALLY = auto()
 64    ERROR = auto()
 65    THROW = auto()
 66
 67    # --- Policy Keywords ---
 68    POLICY = auto()
 69    WHEN = auto()
 70    ALLOW = auto()
 71    DENY = auto()
 72    ESCALATE = auto()
 73
 74    # --- Declaration Keywords ---
 75    ENUM = auto()
 76    STRUCT = auto()
 77    INTERFACE = auto()
 78    SYMBOL = auto()
 79
 80    # --- Shadow Thirst Keywords ---
 81    MUTATION = auto()
 82    VALIDATED_CANONICAL = auto()
 83    INVARIANT = auto()
 84    SHADOW = auto()
 85    CANONICAL = auto()
 86    PROMOTE = auto()
 87    REJECT = auto()
 88    GOVERNED = auto()
 89    REQUIRES = auto()
 90    ENSURES = auto()
 91    CORE = auto()
 92    STRICT = auto()
 93    PURE = auto()
 94    MODULE = auto()
 95
 96    # --- Literals ---
 97    INT = auto()
 98    FLOAT = auto()
 99    STRING = auto()
100    BOOL_TRUE = auto()
101    BOOL_FALSE = auto()
102    NONE = auto()
103    ERROR_LITERAL = auto()
104
105    # --- Delimiters ---
106    LPAREN = auto()
107    RPAREN = auto()
108    LBRACE = auto()
109    RBRACE = auto()
110    LBRACKET = auto()
111    RBRACKET = auto()
112    COMMA = auto()
113    DOT = auto()
114    COLON = auto()
115    COLONEQ = auto()    # := (define-and-assign)
116    SEMICOLON = auto()
117    EQ = auto()
118
119    # --- Multi-char Operators ---
120    ARROW = auto()      # ->
121    PIPE = auto()        # |
122    BACKSLASH = auto()   # \ (lambda)
123    HATHAT = auto()      # ^ (AND-combine)
124    PIPEPIPE = auto()    # || (OR-combine)
125
126    # --- Single-char Operators ---
127    PLUS = auto()
128    MINUS = auto()
129    STAR = auto()
130    SLASH = auto()
131    PERCENT = auto()
132    EQEQ = auto()
133    NE = auto()
134    LT = auto()
135    GT = auto()
136    LE = auto()
137    GE = auto()
138    AND = auto()
139    OR = auto()
140    NOT = auto()
141    ASSIGN = auto()
142
143    # --- Others ---
144    IDENTIFIER = auto()
145    EOF = auto()
DRINK = <TokenType.DRINK: 1>
POUR = <TokenType.POUR: 2>
SIP = <TokenType.SIP: 3>
THIRSTY = <TokenType.THIRSTY: 4>
HYDRATED = <TokenType.HYDRATED: 5>
THIRST = <TokenType.THIRST: 6>
QUENCH = <TokenType.QUENCH: 7>
REFILL = <TokenType.REFILL: 8>
TIMES = <TokenType.TIMES: 9>
GLASS = <TokenType.GLASS: 10>
RESERVOIR = <TokenType.RESERVOIR: 11>
WELL = <TokenType.WELL: 12>
OF = <TokenType.OF: 13>
FLOOD = <TokenType.FLOOD: 14>
DRIP = <TokenType.DRIP: 15>
EVAPORATE = <TokenType.EVAPORATE: 16>
CONDENSE = <TokenType.CONDENSE: 17>
FOUNTAIN = <TokenType.FOUNTAIN: 18>
RETURN = <TokenType.RETURN: 19>
PARCHED = <TokenType.PARCHED: 20>
QUENCHED = <TokenType.QUENCHED: 21>
EMPTY = <TokenType.EMPTY: 22>
MUT = <TokenType.MUT: 23>
IN = <TokenType.IN: 24>
LET = <TokenType.LET: 25>
FOR = <TokenType.FOR: 26>
IMPORT = <TokenType.IMPORT: 27>
FROM = <TokenType.FROM: 28>
AS = <TokenType.AS: 29>
SHIELD = <TokenType.SHIELD: 30>
SANITIZE = <TokenType.SANITIZE: 31>
ARMOR = <TokenType.ARMOR: 32>
MORPH = <TokenType.MORPH: 33>
DETECT = <TokenType.DETECT: 34>
DEFEND = <TokenType.DEFEND: 35>

THIS = <TokenType.THIS: 37>

PUBLIC = <TokenType.PUBLIC: 39>

CLEANUP = <TokenType.CLEANUP: 43>

ERROR = <TokenType.ERROR: 45>

POLICY = <TokenType.POLICY: 47>
WHEN = <TokenType.WHEN: 48>
ALLOW = <TokenType.ALLOW: 49>
DENY = <TokenType.DENY: 50>
ESCALATE = <TokenType.ESCALATE: 51>
ENUM = <TokenType.ENUM: 52>
STRUCT = <TokenType.STRUCT: 53>
INTERFACE = <TokenType.INTERFACE: 54>
SYMBOL = <TokenType.SYMBOL: 55>
MUTATION = <TokenType.MUTATION: 56>
VALIDATED_CANONICAL = <TokenType.VALIDATED_CANONICAL: 57>
INVARIANT = <TokenType.INVARIANT: 58>
SHADOW = <TokenType.SHADOW: 59>
CANONICAL = <TokenType.CANONICAL: 60>
PROMOTE = <TokenType.PROMOTE: 61>
REJECT = <TokenType.REJECT: 62>
GOVERNED = <TokenType.GOVERNED: 63>
REQUIRES = <TokenType.REQUIRES: 64>
ENSURES = <TokenType.ENSURES: 65>
CORE = <TokenType.CORE: 66>
STRICT = <TokenType.STRICT: 67>
PURE = <TokenType.PURE: 68>
MODULE = <TokenType.MODULE: 69>
INT = <TokenType.INT: 70>
FLOAT = <TokenType.FLOAT: 71>
STRING = <TokenType.STRING: 72>
BOOL_TRUE = <TokenType.BOOL_TRUE: 73>
BOOL_FALSE = <TokenType.BOOL_FALSE: 74>
NONE = <TokenType.NONE: 75>
ERROR_LITERAL = <TokenType.ERROR_LITERAL: 76>
LPAREN = <TokenType.LPAREN: 77>
RPAREN = <TokenType.RPAREN: 78>
LBRACE = <TokenType.LBRACE: 79>
RBRACE = <TokenType.RBRACE: 80>
LBRACKET = <TokenType.LBRACKET: 81>
RBRACKET = <TokenType.RBRACKET: 82>
COMMA = <TokenType.COMMA: 83>
DOT = <TokenType.DOT: 84>
COLON = <TokenType.COLON: 85>
COLONEQ = <TokenType.COLONEQ: 86>
SEMICOLON = <TokenType.SEMICOLON: 87>
EQ = <TokenType.EQ: 88>
ARROW = <TokenType.ARROW: 89>
PIPE = <TokenType.PIPE: 90>
BACKSLASH = <TokenType.BACKSLASH: 91>
HATHAT = <TokenType.HATHAT: 92>

PLUS = <TokenType.PLUS: 94>

STAR = <TokenType.STAR: 96>
SLASH = <TokenType.SLASH: 97>
PERCENT = <TokenType.PERCENT: 98>
EQEQ = <TokenType.EQEQ: 99>
NE = <TokenType.NE: 100>
LT = <TokenType.LT: 101>
GT = <TokenType.GT: 102>
LE = <TokenType.LE: 103>
GE = <TokenType.GE: 104>
AND = <TokenType.AND: 105>

KEYWORDS = {'drink': <TokenType.DRINK: 1>, 'pour': <TokenType.POUR: 2>, 'sip': <TokenType.SIP: 3>, 'thirsty': <TokenType.THIRSTY: 4>, 'hydrated': <TokenType.HYDRATED: 5>, 'thirst': <TokenType.THIRST: 6>, 'quench': <TokenType.QUENCH: 7>, 'refill': <TokenType.REFILL: 8>, 'times': <TokenType.TIMES: 9>, 'glass': <TokenType.GLASS: 10>, 'reservoir': <TokenType.RESERVOIR: 11>, 'well': <TokenType.WELL: 12>, 'of': <TokenType.OF: 13>, 'flood': <TokenType.FLOOD: 14>, 'drip': <TokenType.DRIP: 15>, 'evaporate': <TokenType.EVAPORATE: 16>, 'condense': <TokenType.CONDENSE: 17>, 'fountain': <TokenType.FOUNTAIN: 18>, 'return': <TokenType.RETURN: 19>, 'parched': <TokenType.PARCHED: 20>, 'quenched': <TokenType.QUENCHED: 21>, 'empty': <TokenType.EMPTY: 22>, 'mut': <TokenType.MUT: 23>, 'in': <TokenType.IN: 24>, 'let': <TokenType.LET: 25>, 'for': <TokenType.FOR: 26>, 'import': <TokenType.IMPORT: 27>, 'from': <TokenType.FROM: 28>, 'as': <TokenType.AS: 29>, 'shield': <TokenType.SHIELD: 30>, 'sanitize': <TokenType.SANITIZE: 31>, 'armor': <TokenType.ARMOR: 32>, 'morph': <TokenType.MORPH: 33>, 'detect': <TokenType.DETECT: 34>, 'defend': <TokenType.DEFEND: 35>, 'cascade': <TokenType.CASCADE: 36>, 'this': <TokenType.THIS: 37>, 'new': <TokenType.NEW: 38>, 'public': <TokenType.PUBLIC: 39>, 'private': <TokenType.PRIVATE: 40>, 'await': <TokenType.AWAIT: 41>, 'spillage': <TokenType.SPILLAGE: 42>, 'cleanup': <TokenType.CLEANUP: 43>, 'finally': <TokenType.FINALLY: 44>, 'error': <TokenType.ERROR: 45>, 'throw': <TokenType.THROW: 46>, 'policy': <TokenType.POLICY: 47>, 'when': <TokenType.WHEN: 48>, 'allow': <TokenType.ALLOW: 49>, 'deny': <TokenType.DENY: 50>, 'escalate': <TokenType.ESCALATE: 51>, 'mutation': <TokenType.MUTATION: 56>, 'validated_canonical': <TokenType.VALIDATED_CANONICAL: 57>, 'invariant': <TokenType.INVARIANT: 58>, 'shadow': <TokenType.SHADOW: 59>, 'canonical': <TokenType.CANONICAL: 60>, 'promote': <TokenType.PROMOTE: 61>, 'reject': <TokenType.REJECT: 62>, 'governed': <TokenType.GOVERNED: 63>, 'requires': <TokenType.REQUIRES: 64>, 'ensures': <TokenType.ENSURES: 65>, 'enum': <TokenType.ENUM: 52>, 'struct': <TokenType.STRUCT: 53>, 'interface': <TokenType.INTERFACE: 54>, 'symbol': <TokenType.SYMBOL: 55>, 'module': <TokenType.MODULE: 69>, 'core': <TokenType.CORE: 66>, 'strict': <TokenType.STRICT: 67>, 'pure': <TokenType.PURE: 68>, 'and': <TokenType.AND: 105>, 'or': <TokenType.OR: 106>, 'not': <TokenType.NOT: 107>, 'true': <TokenType.BOOL_TRUE: 73>, 'false': <TokenType.BOOL_FALSE: 74>, 'none': <TokenType.NONE: 75>}
class Lexer: 
 10class Lexer:
 11    """Tokenizes Thirsty-Lang source code into a list of Tokens."""
 12
 13    def __init__(self, source: str):
 14        self.source = source
 15        self.tokens: list[Token] = []
 16        self.errors: list[Diagnostic] = []
 17        self.start = 0       # start of current lexeme
 18        self.current = 0     # current position
 19        self.line = 1
 20        self.col = 1
 21        self.line_start = 0  # column offset of line start for span tracking
 22
 23    def lex(self) -> list[Token]:
 24        """Tokenize the entire source and return tokens."""
 25        while not self._is_at_end():
 26            self.start = self.current
 27            self._scan_token()
 28        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.EOF, "", self.line, self.col))
 29        return self.tokens
 30
 31    def _is_at_end(self) -> bool:
 32        return self.current >= len(self.source)
 33
 34    def _advance(self) -> str:
 35        ch = self.source[self.current]
 36        self.current += 1
 37        self.col += 1
 38        return ch
 39
 40    def _peek(self) -> str:
 41        if self._is_at_end():
 42            return "\0"
 43        return self.source[self.current]
 44
 45    def _peek_next(self) -> str:
 46        if self.current + 1 >= len(self.source):
 47            return "\0"
 48        return self.source[self.current + 1]
 49
 50    def _match(self, expected: str) -> bool:
 51        if self._is_at_end() or self.source[self.current] != expected:
 52            return False
 53        self.current += 1
 54        self.col += 1
 55        return True
 56
 57    def _add_token(self, token_type: TokenType, lexeme: str | None = None):
 58        text = lexeme if lexeme is not None else self.source[self.start:self.current]
 59        self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, text, self.line, self.start - self.line_start + 1))
 60
 61    def _error(self, code: str, message: str):
 62        col = self.start - self.line_start + 1
 63        self.errors.append(Diagnostic(
 64            code=code, message=message,
 65            span=(self.line, col, self.line, col),
 66            severity="error"
 67        ))
 68
 69    def _scan_token(self):
 70        ch = self._advance()
 71        handlers = {
 72            "(": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.LPAREN),
 73            ")": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.RPAREN),
 74            ":": lambda: self._handle_colon(),
 75            "{": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.LBRACE),
 76            "}": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.RBRACE),
 77            "[": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.LBRACKET),
 78            "]": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.RBRACKET),
 79            ",": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.COMMA),
 80            ".": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.DOT),
 81            ";": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.SEMICOLON),
 82            "+": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.PLUS),
 83            "-": lambda: self._handle_minus(),
 84            "*": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.STAR),
 85            "%": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.PERCENT),
 86            "|": lambda: self._handle_pipe(),
 87            "^": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.HATHAT),
 88            "\\": lambda: self._add_token(TokenType.BACKSLASH),
 89            "=": lambda: self._handle_equals(),
 90            "!": lambda: self._handle_bang(),
 91            "<": lambda: self._handle_less(),
 92            ">": lambda: self._handle_greater(),
 93            "/": lambda: self._handle_slash(),
 94            '"': lambda: self._string('"'),
 95            "'": lambda: self._string("'"),
 96            "\n": lambda: self._handle_newline(),
 97            " ": lambda: None,
 98            "\r": lambda: None,
 99            "\t": lambda: None,
100        }
101        handler = handlers.get(ch)
102        if handler:
103            handler()
104        elif ch.isdigit() or (ch == "0" and self._peek() in "xXbBoO"):
105            self._number(ch)
106        elif ch.isalpha() or ch == "_":
107            self._identifier()
108        else:
109            self._error("E001", f"Unrecognized character: '{ch}'")
110
111    def _handle_newline(self):
112        self.line += 1
113        self.line_start = self.current
114
115    def _handle_minus(self):
116        if self._match(">"):
117            self._add_token(TokenType.ARROW, "->")
118        else:
119            self._add_token(TokenType.MINUS)
120
121    def _handle_colon(self):
122        if self._match("="):
123            self._add_token(TokenType.COLONEQ, ":=")
124        else:
125            self._add_token(TokenType.COLON)
126
127    def _handle_pipe(self):
128        if self._match("|"):
129            self._add_token(TokenType.PIPEPIPE, "||")
130        elif self._match(">"):
131            # The pipe operator is documented as `|>`; accept it as a single
132            # token (bare `|` is also accepted as the same operator).
133            self._add_token(TokenType.PIPE, "|>")
134        else:
135            self._add_token(TokenType.PIPE)
136
137    def _handle_equals(self):
138        if self._match("="):
139            self._add_token(TokenType.EQEQ, "==")
140        else:
141            self._add_token(TokenType.ASSIGN)
142
143    def _handle_bang(self):
144        if self._match("="):
145            self._add_token(TokenType.NE, "!=")
146        else:
147            self._error("E001", "Unrecognized character: '!' (use 'not' for negation)")
148
149    def _handle_less(self):
150        if self._match("="):
151            self._add_token(TokenType.LE, "<=")
152        else:
153            self._add_token(TokenType.LT)
154
155    def _handle_greater(self):
156        if self._match("="):
157            self._add_token(TokenType.GE, ">=")
158        else:
159            self._add_token(TokenType.GT)
160
161    def _handle_slash(self):
162        if self._match("/"):
163            # Line comment: consume until end of line
164            while self._peek() != "\n" and not self._is_at_end():
165                self._advance()
166        elif self._match("*"):
167            # Block comment: consume until */
168            depth = 1
169            while depth > 0 and not self._is_at_end():
170                if self._peek() == "/" and self._peek_next() == "*":
171                    self._advance()
172                    self._advance()
173                    depth += 1
174                elif self._peek() == "*" and self._peek_next() == "/":
175                    self._advance()
176                    self._advance()
177                    depth -= 1
178                elif self._peek() == "\n":
179                    self._advance()
180                    self.line += 1
181                    self.line_start = self.current
182                else:
183                    self._advance()
184            if depth > 0:
185                self._error("E003", "Unterminated block comment")
186        else:
187            self._add_token(TokenType.SLASH)
188
189    def _identifier(self):
190        while self._peek().isalnum() or self._peek() == "_":
191            self._advance()
192        text = self.source[self.start:self.current]
193        token_type = KEYWORDS.get(text, TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
194        self._add_token(token_type, text)
195
196    def _number(self, first: str):
197        # Detect prefix-based numbers
198        if first == "0" and not self._is_at_end():
199            nxt = self.source[self.current]
200            if nxt in "xX":
201                self._advance()
202                return self._hex_number()
203            elif nxt in "bB":
204                self._advance()
205                return self._binary_number()
206            elif nxt in "oO":
207                self._advance()
208                return self._octal_number()
209
210        # Regular int or float
211        while self._peek().isdigit():
212            self._advance()
213
214        is_float = False
215        if self._peek() == "." and self._peek_next().isdigit():
216            is_float = True
217            self._advance()
218            while self._peek().isdigit():
219                self._advance()
220
221        if self._peek() in "eE":
222            is_float = True
223            self._advance()
224            if self._peek() in "+-":
225                self._advance()
226            while self._peek().isdigit():
227                self._advance()
228
229        text = self.source[self.start:self.current]
230        if is_float:
231            self._add_token(TokenType.FLOAT, text)
232        else:
233            self._add_token(TokenType.INT, text)
234
235    def _hex_number(self):
236        while self._peek().isalnum():
237            self._advance()
238        text = self.source[self.start:self.current]
239        self._add_token(TokenType.INT, text)
240
241    def _binary_number(self):
242        while self._peek() in "01":
243            self._advance()
244        text = self.source[self.start:self.current]
245        self._add_token(TokenType.INT, text)
246
247    def _octal_number(self):
248        while self._peek() in "01234567":
249            self._advance()
250        text = self.source[self.start:self.current]
251        self._add_token(TokenType.INT, text)
252
253    def _string(self, quote: str):
254        text_parts = []
255        while self._peek() != quote and not self._is_at_end():
256            if self._peek() == "\n":
257                self.line += 1
258                self.line_start = self.current + 1
259            if self._peek() == "\\":
260                self._advance()  # consume backslash
261                esc = self._advance()
262                esc_map = {"n": "\n", "t": "\t", "\\": "\\", '"': '"', "'": "'", "0": "\0"}
263                text_parts.append(esc_map.get(esc, esc))
264            else:
265                text_parts.append(self._advance())
266
267        if self._is_at_end():
268            self._error("E002", "Unterminated string literal")
269            text = "".join(text_parts)
270        else:
271            self._advance()  # consume closing quote
272            text = "".join(text_parts)
273
274        self._add_token(TokenType.STRING, text)
275
276    def get_errors(self) -> list[Diagnostic]:
277        return self.errors

Tokenizes Thirsty-Lang source code into a list of Tokens.

Lexer(source: str) 
13    def __init__(self, source: str):
14        self.source = source
15        self.tokens: list[Token] = []
16        self.errors: list[Diagnostic] = []
17        self.start = 0       # start of current lexeme
18        self.current = 0     # current position
19        self.line = 1
20        self.col = 1
21        self.line_start = 0  # column offset of line start for span tracking
source
tokens: list[Token]
errors: list[Diagnostic]
start
current
line
col
line_start
def lex(self) -> list[Token]: 
23    def lex(self) -> list[Token]:
24        """Tokenize the entire source and return tokens."""
25        while not self._is_at_end():
26            self.start = self.current
27            self._scan_token()
28        self.tokens.append(Token(TokenType.EOF, "", self.line, self.col))
29        return self.tokens

Tokenize the entire source and return tokens.

def get_errors(self) -> list[Diagnostic]: 
276    def get_errors(self) -> list[Diagnostic]:
277        return self.errors
@dataclass
class Program: 
388@dataclass
389class Program:
390    stmts: list
391    header: ModuleHeader | None = None
392    span: tuple = (0, 0, 0, 0)
393    # Set when a governed module had parse errors: the parser discards all
394    # recovered statements (fail-closed) and the interpreter refuses to run it.
395    parse_failed: bool = False
Program( stmts: list, header: ModuleHeader | None = None, span: tuple = (0, 0, 0, 0), parse_failed: bool = False)
stmts: list
header: ModuleHeader | None = None
span: tuple = (0, 0, 0, 0)
parse_failed: bool = False
@dataclass
class ModuleHeader: 
381@dataclass
382class ModuleHeader:
383    name: str
384    mode: str  # "core" or "governed"
385    span: tuple
ModuleHeader(name: str, mode: str, span: tuple)
name: str
mode: str
span: tuple
@dataclass
class FunctionDecl(utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt): 
321@dataclass
322class FunctionDecl(Stmt):
323    """glass name(params) -> return_type { body }"""
324    name: str
325    params: list  # list of (name, type_str)
326    return_type: str | None  # optional return type annotation
327    body: Stmt

glass name(params) -> return_type { body }

return_type: str | None
body: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt

class ClassDecl(utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt):

331class ClassDecl(Stmt):

334    methods: list

fountain name { fields, methods }

methods: list
fields: list

class VariableDecl(utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt):

210    init_expr: Expr | None
211    is_mut: bool = False

drink name: type = expr OR drink mut name = expr

308    body: Stmt

morph name(params) { body }

body: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt

@dataclass
class GovernedFunctionDecl(utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt): 
356@dataclass
357class GovernedFunctionDecl(Stmt):
358    """Governed function with a `requires` precondition.
359
360    ``requires_annotation`` holds the source-text form of the precondition
361    (used by the formatter and the CLI module-info path).
362    ``requires_expr`` holds the parsed precondition AST that the interpreter
363    evaluates at call time to enforce governance.
364    """
365    name: str
366    params: list
367    return_type: str | None
368    body: Stmt
369    requires_annotation: str | None = None
370    requires_expr: Expr | None = None
371    # Postcondition: checked after the body with ``result`` bound to the return.
372    ensures_annotation: str | None = None
373    ensures_expr: Expr | None = None
374    # Invariant: a predicate enforced at both call entry and exit.
375    invariant_annotation: str | None = None
376    invariant_expr: Expr | None = None

Governed function with a requires precondition.

requires_annotation holds the source-text form of the precondition (used by the formatter and the CLI module-info path). requires_expr holds the parsed precondition AST that the interpreter evaluates at call time to enforce governance.

GovernedFunctionDecl( span: tuple, name: str, params: list, return_type: str | None, body: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt, requires_annotation: str | None = None, requires_expr: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Expr | None = None, ensures_annotation: str | None = None, ensures_expr: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Expr | None = None, invariant_annotation: str | None = None, invariant_expr: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Expr | None = None)
name: str
params: list
return_type: str | None
body: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt
requires_annotation: str | None = None
requires_expr: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Expr | None = None
ensures_annotation: str | None = None
ensures_expr: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Expr | None = None
invariant_annotation: str | None = None
invariant_expr: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Expr | None = None

279    handlers: list = field(default_factory=list)  # list of (error_type_expr, handler_block)

try/spillage block with error handlers.

SpillageStmt( span: tuple, body: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt, handlers: list = <factory>)
body: utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt
handlers: list
