@dataclass
class ShadowThirstMutation(utf.thirsty_lang.ast.Stmt): 
400@dataclass
401class ShadowThirstMutation(Stmt):
402    """mutation name { validated_canonical { shadow { ... } invariant { ... } canonical { ... } } }"""
403    name: str
404    shadow_block: 'BlockStmt | None' = None
405    invariant_block: 'BlockStmt | None' = None
406    canonical_block: 'BlockStmt | None' = None

mutation name { validated_canonical { shadow { ... } invariant { ... } canonical { ... } } }

ShadowThirstMutation( span: tuple, name: str, shadow_block: BlockStmt | None = None, invariant_block: BlockStmt | None = None, canonical_block: BlockStmt | None = None)
name: str
shadow_block: BlockStmt | None = None
invariant_block: BlockStmt | None = None
canonical_block: BlockStmt | None = None
class Parser: 
  89class Parser:
  90    """Recursive descent parser for Thirsty-Lang."""
  91
  92    def __init__(self, tokens: list[Token]):
  93        self.tokens = tokens
  94        self.current = 0
  95        self.errors: list[Diagnostic] = []
  96
  97    def parse(self) -> Program:
  98        """Parse the token stream into a Program AST."""
  99        header = self._parse_module_header()
 100        stmts = []
 101        while not self._is_at_end():
 102            try:
 103                stmt = self._parse_statement()
 104                if stmt is not None:
 105                    stmts.append(stmt)
 106                else:
 107                    self._advance()  # Skip unrecognized
 108            except Exception as e:
 109                self.errors.append(make_error("E901", span=self._current_span(),
 110                                              detail=str(e)))
 111                self._synchronize()
 112        span = self._span_range()
 113        # Fail closed for governed modules: if parsing produced any errors, the
 114        # recovered statements cannot be trusted to preserve author intent, so
 115        # discard them entirely rather than letting recovery smuggle executable
 116        # statements past a malformed boundary. The interpreter refuses to run a
 117        # program flagged this way.
 118        if self.errors and header is not None and header.mode == "governed":
 119            return Program(stmts=[], header=header, span=span,
 120                           parse_failed=True)
 121        return Program(stmts=stmts, header=header, span=span)
 122
 123    def _is_at_end(self) -> bool:
 124        return self._peek().type == TokenType.EOF
 125
 126    def _peek(self) -> Token:
 127        return self.tokens[self.current]
 128
 129    def _previous(self) -> Token:
 130        return self.tokens[self.current - 1]
 131
 132    def _advance(self) -> Token:
 133        if not self._is_at_end():
 134            self.current += 1
 135        return self._previous()
 136
 137    def _check(self, *types: TokenType) -> bool:
 138        if self._is_at_end():
 139            return False
 140        return self._peek().type in types
 141
 142    def _match(self, *types: TokenType) -> bool:
 143        for t in types:
 144            if self._check(t):
 145                self._advance()
 146                return True
 147        return False
 148
 149    def _expect(self, token_type: TokenType, error_code: str = "E901", **kwargs) -> Token:
 150        if self._check(token_type):
 151            return self._advance()
 152        msg = f"Expected {token_type.name} but got {self._peek().type.name}"
 153        span = self._current_span()
 154        self.errors.append(make_error(error_code, span=span, detail=msg))
 155        # Return a synthetic token
 156        return Token(token_type, "", self._peek().line, self._peek().col)
 157
 158    def _current_span(self) -> tuple:
 159        t = self._peek()
 160        return (t.line, t.col, t.line, t.col + len(t.lexeme))
 161
 162    def _span_range(self) -> tuple:
 163        if len(self.tokens) > 1:
 164            first = self.tokens[0]
 165            last = self.tokens[-1]
 166            return (first.line, first.col, last.line, last.col + len(last.lexeme))
 167        return (1, 1, 1, 1)
 168
 169    def _span(self, start_token: Token | None = None) -> tuple:
 170        if start_token is None:
 171            start_token = self.tokens[0]
 172        end_token = self._previous()
 173        return (start_token.line, start_token.col,
 174                end_token.line, end_token.col + len(end_token.lexeme))
 175
 176    def _synchronize(self):
 177        """Skip tokens until a statement boundary is found (error recovery)."""
 178        while not self._is_at_end():
 179            if self._previous().type == TokenType.SEMICOLON:
 180                return
 181            t = self._peek().type
 182            if t in (TokenType.DRINK, TokenType.POUR, TokenType.SIP,
 183                     TokenType.THIRSTY, TokenType.REFILL, TokenType.RETURN,
 184                     TokenType.IMPORT, TokenType.GLASS, TokenType.FOUNTAIN,
 185                     TokenType.SPILLAGE, TokenType.SHIELD, TokenType.MUTATION,
 186                     TokenType.REQUIRES, TokenType.RBRACE, TokenType.EOF):
 187                return
 188            self._advance()
 189
 190    # === Module Header ===
 191
 192    def _parse_module_header(self) -> ModuleHeader | None:
 193        if not self._match(TokenType.MODULE):
 194            return None
 195        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901", detail="Expected module name")
 196        self._expect(TokenType.COLON, "E901", detail="Expected ':' after module name")
 197        self._peek()
 198        mode = "core"
 199        if self._match(TokenType.GOVERNED):
 200            mode = "governed"
 201        elif self._match(TokenType.CORE):
 202            mode = "core"
 203        elif self._match(TokenType.STRICT):
 204            mode = "strict"
 205        elif self._match(TokenType.PURE):
 206            mode = "pure"
 207        else:
 208            self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 209                         detail="Expected mode (core/governed/strict/pure)")
 210        span = self._span(name_token)
 211        return ModuleHeader(name=name_token.lexeme, mode=mode, span=span)
 212
 213    # === Statement Parsing ===
 214
 215    def _parse_statement(self) -> object | None:
 216        t = self._peek().type
 217
 218        if t == TokenType.DRINK:
 219            return self._parse_variable_decl()
 220        elif t == TokenType.LET:
 221            return self._parse_let_decl()
 222        elif t == TokenType.FOR:
 223            return self._parse_for_stmt()
 224        elif t == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self._peek_next(1).type == TokenType.COLONEQ:
 225            return self._parse_walrus_decl()
 226        elif t == TokenType.POUR:
 227            return self._parse_pour_stmt()
 228        elif t == TokenType.SIP:
 229            return self._parse_sip_stmt()
 230        elif t == TokenType.THIRSTY:
 231            return self._parse_if_stmt()
 232        elif t == TokenType.REFILL:
 233            return self._parse_refill_stmt()
 234        elif t == TokenType.TIMES:
 235            return self._parse_times_stmt()
 236        elif t == TokenType.RETURN:
 237            return self._parse_return_stmt()
 238        elif t == TokenType.IMPORT:
 239            return self._parse_import_stmt()
 240        elif t == TokenType.LBRACE:
 241            return self._parse_block()
 242        elif t == TokenType.GLASS:
 243            return self._parse_function_decl()
 244        elif t == TokenType.FOUNTAIN:
 245            return self._parse_class_decl()
 246        elif t == TokenType.SPILLAGE:
 247            return self._parse_spillage_stmt()
 248        elif t == TokenType.CLEANUP:
 249            return self._parse_cleanup_stmt()
 250        elif t == TokenType.THROW:
 251            return self._parse_throw_stmt()
 252        elif t == TokenType.SHIELD:
 253            return self._parse_security_block("shield")
 254        elif t == TokenType.SANITIZE:
 255            return self._parse_sanitize_expr()
 256        elif t == TokenType.ARMOR:
 257            return self._parse_armor_expr()
 258        elif t == TokenType.MORPH:
 259            return self._parse_morph_def()
 260        elif t == TokenType.DETECT:
 261            return self._parse_security_block("detect")
 262        elif t == TokenType.DEFEND:
 263            return self._parse_defend_strat()
 264        elif t == TokenType.CASCADE:
 265            return self._parse_cascade_call()
 266        elif t == TokenType.NEW:
 267            return self._parse_new_expr()
 268        elif t == TokenType.ENUM:
 269            return self._parse_enum_decl()
 270        elif t == TokenType.STRUCT:
 271            return self._parse_struct_decl()
 272        elif t == TokenType.INTERFACE:
 273            return self._parse_interface_decl()
 274        elif t == TokenType.MUTATION:
 275            return self._parse_shadow_thirst_mutation()
 276        elif t == TokenType.SYMBOL:
 277            return self._parse_tscg_symbol()
 278        elif t == TokenType.PIPE:
 279            return self._parse_pipe_block_stmt()
 280        else:
 281            return self._parse_expr_statement()
 282
 283    def _parse_pipe_block_stmt(self) -> ExprStmt:
 284        """Parse | [>] expression — pipe block statement.
 285
 286        At statement level, pipes a value through a pipeline expression.
 287        The pipe symbol introduces an expression that may contain further
 288        infix pipe operators (| for PipeExpr).
 289        """
 290        start = self._advance()  # consume PIPE
 291        self._match(TokenType.GT)  # optional > for |>
 292        expr = self._parse_expr()
 293        self._expect(TokenType.SEMICOLON, "E901", detail="Expected ';' after pipe expression")
 294        return ExprStmt(expr=expr, span=self._span(start))
 295
 296    def _parse_block(self) -> BlockStmt:
 297        start = self._advance()  # consume {
 298        stmts = []
 299        while not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self._is_at_end():
 300            stmt = self._parse_statement()
 301            if stmt:
 302                stmts.append(stmt)
 303            else:
 304                self._advance()
 305        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '}' to close block")
 306        return BlockStmt(statements=stmts, span=self._span(start))
 307
 308    def _parse_variable_decl(self) -> VariableDecl:
 309        start = self._advance()  # drink
 310        is_mut = self._match(TokenType.MUT)
 311        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 312                                  detail="Expected variable name after 'drink'")
 313        var_type = None
 314        if self._match(TokenType.COLON):
 315            type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 316                                      detail="Expected type after ':'")
 317            var_type = type_token.lexeme
 318        init_expr = None
 319        if self._match(TokenType.ASSIGN):
 320            init_expr = self._parse_expr()
 321        elif self._match(TokenType.EQ):
 322            init_expr = self._parse_expr()
 323        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 324        return VariableDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, var_type=var_type,
 325                            init_expr=init_expr, is_mut=is_mut, span=self._span(start))
 326
 327    def _parse_let_decl(self) -> VariableDecl:
 328        """let name [: type] = expr — immutable binding (analogous to `drink`)."""
 329        start = self._advance()  # let
 330        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 331                                  detail="Expected variable name after 'let'")
 332        var_type = None
 333        if self._match(TokenType.COLON):
 334            type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 335                                      detail="Expected type after ':'")
 336            var_type = type_token.lexeme
 337        init_expr = None
 338        if self._match(TokenType.ASSIGN):
 339            init_expr = self._parse_expr()
 340        elif self._match(TokenType.EQ):
 341            init_expr = self._parse_expr()
 342        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 343        return VariableDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, var_type=var_type,
 344                            init_expr=init_expr, is_mut=False,
 345                            span=self._span(start))
 346
 347    def _parse_walrus_decl(self) -> VariableDecl:
 348        """name := expr — define-and-assign a new mutable binding."""
 349        name_token = self._advance()  # identifier
 350        self._advance()  # :=
 351        init_expr = self._parse_expr()
 352        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 353        return VariableDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, var_type=None,
 354                            init_expr=init_expr, is_mut=True,
 355                            span=self._span(name_token))
 356
 357    def _parse_for_stmt(self) -> ForStmt:
 358        """for [(] x in iterable [)] { body } — keyword form of refill(x in xs)."""
 359        start = self._advance()  # for
 360        has_paren = self._match(TokenType.LPAREN)
 361        var_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 362                                 detail="Expected loop variable after 'for'")
 363        self._expect(TokenType.IN, "E901",
 364                     detail="Expected 'in' after loop variable")
 365        iterable = self._parse_expr()
 366        if has_paren:
 367            self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901",
 368                         detail="Expected ')' after iterable")
 369        body = self._parse_block()
 370        return ForStmt(
 371            variable=Identifier(name=var_token.lexeme,
 372                                span=self._span(var_token)),
 373            iterable=iterable, body=body, span=self._span(start))
 374
 375    def _parse_pour_stmt(self) -> PourStmt:
 376        start = self._advance()
 377        value = self._parse_expr()
 378        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 379        return PourStmt(value=value, span=self._span(start))
 380
 381    def _parse_sip_stmt(self) -> SipStmt:
 382        start = self._advance()
 383        target = self._parse_expr()
 384        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 385        return SipStmt(target=target, span=self._span(start))
 386
 387    def _parse_if_stmt(self) -> IfStmt:
 388        start = self._advance()  # thirsty
 389        self._expect(TokenType.LPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected '(' after 'thirsty'")
 390        condition = self._parse_expr()
 391        self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after condition")
 392        then_block = self._parse_block()
 393        else_block: Stmt | None = None
 394        if self._match(TokenType.HYDRATED):
 395            if self._check(TokenType.THIRSTY):
 396                else_block = self._parse_if_stmt()  # else if
 397            else:
 398                else_block = self._parse_block()
 399        return IfStmt(condition=condition, then_block=then_block,
 400                      else_block=else_block, span=self._span(start))
 401
 402    def _parse_refill_stmt(self) -> WhileStmt | ForStmt | BlockStmt:
 403        start = self._advance()  # refill
 404        self._expect(TokenType.LPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected '(' after 'refill'")
 405        # for-each loop: refill(var in iterable)
 406        if self._check(TokenType.IDENTIFIER) and self._peek_next(1).type == TokenType.IN:
 407            var_token = self._advance()
 408            self._advance()  # in
 409            iterable = self._parse_expr()
 410            self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after iterable")
 411            body = self._parse_block()
 412            return ForStmt(variable=Identifier(name=var_token.lexeme, span=self._span(var_token)),
 413                           iterable=iterable, body=body, span=self._span(start))
 414        # C-style for: refill(init; cond; step). A declaration initializer
 415        # (drink/let) unambiguously marks this form; an expression initializer
 416        # is distinguished from a plain while condition by the trailing ';'.
 417        if self._check(TokenType.DRINK):
 418            init = self._parse_variable_decl()  # consumes its own optional ';'
 419            return self._finish_cstyle_for(start, init)
 420        if self._check(TokenType.LET):
 421            init = self._parse_let_decl()
 422            return self._finish_cstyle_for(start, init)
 423        first = self._parse_expr()
 424        if self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON):
 425            expr_init = first if isinstance(first, AssignStmt) else ExprStmt(
 426                expr=first, span=first.span)
 427            return self._finish_cstyle_for(start, expr_init)
 428        # While loop: refill(condition)
 429        self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after condition")
 430        body = self._parse_block()
 431        return WhileStmt(condition=first, body=body, span=self._span(start))
 432
 433    def _finish_cstyle_for(self, start, init) -> BlockStmt:
 434        """Parse `cond; step) { body }` after the init clause and desugar the
 435        C-style ``refill(init; cond; step)`` into an init statement followed by
 436        a while loop whose body ends with the step statement."""
 437        # The loop counter is reassigned by the step clause, so a declaration
 438        # initializer is implicitly mutable (no `mut` keyword required).
 439        if isinstance(init, VariableDecl):
 440            init.is_mut = True
 441        condition = self._parse_expr()
 442        self._expect(TokenType.SEMICOLON, "E901",
 443                     detail="Expected ';' after loop condition")
 444        step_expr = self._parse_expr()
 445        step = step_expr if isinstance(step_expr, AssignStmt) else ExprStmt(
 446            expr=step_expr, span=step_expr.span)
 447        self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901",
 448                     detail="Expected ')' after loop step")
 449        body = self._parse_block()
 450        inner = BlockStmt(statements=list(body.statements) + [step],
 451                          span=body.span)
 452        loop = WhileStmt(condition=condition, body=inner,
 453                         span=self._span(start))
 454        return BlockStmt(statements=[init, loop], span=self._span(start))
 455
 456    def _parse_times_stmt(self) -> TimesStmt:
 457        start = self._advance()  # times
 458        count = self._parse_expr()
 459        body = self._parse_block()
 460        return TimesStmt(count=count, body=body, span=self._span(start))
 461
 462    def _parse_return_stmt(self) -> ReturnStmt:
 463        start = self._advance()
 464        value = None
 465        if not self._check(TokenType.SEMICOLON) and not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE):
 466            value = self._parse_expr()
 467        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 468        return ReturnStmt(value=value, span=self._span(start))
 469
 470    def _parse_import_stmt(self) -> ImportStmt:
 471        start = self._advance()
 472        path_token = self._expect(TokenType.STRING, "E901",
 473                                  detail="Expected module path string after 'import'")
 474        alias = None
 475        if self._match(TokenType.AS):
 476            alias_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 477                                       detail="Expected alias name after 'as'")
 478            alias = alias_token.lexeme
 479        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 480        return ImportStmt(module_path=path_token.lexeme, alias=alias, span=self._span(start))
 481
 482    def _parse_function_decl(self) -> FunctionDecl | GovernedFunctionDecl:
 483        start = self._advance()  # glass
 484        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 485                                  detail="Expected function name after 'glass'")
 486        params = self._parse_params()
 487        return_type = None
 488        if self._match(TokenType.ARROW):
 489            type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 490                                      detail="Expected return type after '->'")
 491            return_type = type_token.lexeme
 492        # Governance clauses (any order): requires / ensures / invariant.
 493        clauses = {}
 494        while self._check(TokenType.REQUIRES, TokenType.ENSURES,
 495                          TokenType.INVARIANT):
 496            kind = self._advance().type
 497            c_start = self.current
 498            expr = self._parse_expr()
 499            text = " ".join(
 500                t.lexeme for t in self.tokens[c_start:self.current])
 501            clauses[kind] = (expr, text)
 502        body = self._parse_block()
 503        if clauses:
 504            req = clauses.get(TokenType.REQUIRES, (None, None))
 505            ens = clauses.get(TokenType.ENSURES, (None, None))
 506            inv = clauses.get(TokenType.INVARIANT, (None, None))
 507            return GovernedFunctionDecl(
 508                name=name_token.lexeme, params=params,
 509                return_type=return_type, body=body,
 510                requires_annotation=req[1], requires_expr=req[0],
 511                ensures_annotation=ens[1], ensures_expr=ens[0],
 512                invariant_annotation=inv[1], invariant_expr=inv[0],
 513                span=self._span(start))
 514        return FunctionDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, params=params,
 515                            return_type=return_type, body=body, span=self._span(start))
 516
 517    def _parse_class_decl(self) -> ClassDecl:
 518        start = self._advance()
 519        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 520                                  detail="Expected class name after 'fountain'")
 521        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for class body")
 522        fields = []
 523        methods = []
 524        while not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self._is_at_end():
 525            if self._check(TokenType.GLASS):
 526                methods.append(self._parse_function_decl())
 527            elif (self._check(TokenType.DRINK)
 528                  or self._check(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)):
 529                # field: [drink [mut]] name [: type] [= default]
 530                self._match(TokenType.DRINK)
 531                self._match(TokenType.MUT)
 532                name_t = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 533                                      detail="Expected field name")
 534                var_type = None
 535                if self._match(TokenType.COLON):
 536                    type_t = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 537                                          detail="Expected field type")
 538                    var_type = type_t.lexeme
 539                default_expr = None
 540                if self._match(TokenType.ASSIGN):
 541                    default_expr = self._parse_expr()
 542                fields.append((name_t.lexeme, var_type, default_expr))
 543                self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 544            else:
 545                self._advance()
 546        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '}' to close class")
 547        return ClassDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, methods=methods,
 548                         fields=fields, span=self._span(start))
 549
 550    def _parse_params(self) -> list:
 551        params = []
 552        self._expect(TokenType.LPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected '(' for parameters")
 553        if not self._check(TokenType.RPAREN):
 554            name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 555                                      detail="Expected parameter name")
 556            param_type = None
 557            if self._match(TokenType.COLON):
 558                type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 559                                          detail="Expected parameter type")
 560                param_type = type_token.lexeme
 561            params.append((name_token.lexeme, param_type))
 562            while self._match(TokenType.COMMA):
 563                name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 564                                          detail="Expected parameter name")
 565                param_type = None
 566                if self._match(TokenType.COLON):
 567                    type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 568                                              detail="Expected parameter type")
 569                    param_type = type_token.lexeme
 570                params.append((name_token.lexeme, param_type))
 571        self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after parameters")
 572        return params
 573
 574    def _parse_spillage_stmt(self) -> SpillageStmt:
 575        start = self._advance()
 576        body = self._parse_block()
 577        handlers = []
 578        while self._match(TokenType.ERROR):
 579            # error [(name)] { handler_block } — the optional binding name
 580            # receives the thrown value inside the handler block.
 581            error_var = None
 582            if self._match(TokenType.LPAREN):
 583                name_token = self._expect(
 584                    TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 585                    detail="Expected error binding name after '('")
 586                error_var = name_token.lexeme
 587                self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901",
 588                             detail="Expected ')' after error binding")
 589            handler_block = self._parse_block()
 590            handlers.append((error_var, handler_block))
 591        return SpillageStmt(body=body, handlers=handlers, span=self._span(start))
 592
 593    def _parse_cleanup_stmt(self) -> CleanupStmt:
 594        start = self._advance()
 595        body = self._parse_block()
 596        self._expect(TokenType.FINALLY, "E901",
 597                      detail="Expected 'finally' after cleanup body")
 598        finalizer = self._parse_block()
 599        return CleanupStmt(body=body, finalizer=finalizer, span=self._span(start))
 600
 601    def _parse_throw_stmt(self) -> ThrowStmt:
 602        start = self._advance()
 603        value = self._parse_expr()
 604        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 605        return ThrowStmt(value=value, span=self._span(start))
 606
 607    def _parse_security_block(self, block_type: str) -> SecurityBlock:
 608        start = self._advance()
 609        body = self._parse_block()
 610        return SecurityBlock(block_type=block_type, body=body, span=self._span(start))
 611
 612    def _parse_sanitize_expr(self) -> SanitizeExpr:
 613        start = self._advance()
 614        expr = self._parse_expr()
 615        return SanitizeExpr(expr=expr, span=self._span(start))
 616
 617    def _parse_armor_expr(self) -> ArmorExpr:
 618        start = self._advance()
 619        expr = self._parse_expr()
 620        return ArmorExpr(expr=expr, span=self._span(start))
 621
 622    def _parse_morph_def(self) -> MorphDef:
 623        start = self._advance()
 624        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 625                                  detail="Expected morph name")
 626        params = self._parse_params()
 627        body = self._parse_block()
 628        return MorphDef(name=name_token.lexeme, params=params,
 629                        body=body, span=self._span(start))
 630
 631    def _parse_defend_strat(self) -> DefendStrat:
 632        start = self._advance()
 633        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 634                                  detail="Expected defend strategy name")
 635        # Parse policy name
 636        policy = ""
 637        if self._match(TokenType.LPAREN):
 638            policy_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 639                                        detail="Expected policy name")
 640            policy = policy_token.lexeme
 641            self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after policy")
 642        # Parse actions block
 643        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for actions")
 644        actions = []
 645        while not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self._is_at_end():
 646            actions.append(self._parse_expr())
 647            self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 648        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '}' after actions")
 649        return DefendStrat(name=name_token.lexeme, policy=policy,
 650                           actions=actions, span=self._span(start))
 651
 652    def _parse_enum_decl(self) -> EnumDecl:
 653        start = self._advance()
 654        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 655                                  detail="Expected enum name")
 656        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for enum variants")
 657        variants = []
 658        if not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE):
 659            var_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 660                                     detail="Expected variant name")
 661            variants.append(var_token.lexeme)
 662            while self._match(TokenType.COMMA):
 663                var_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 664                                         detail="Expected variant name")
 665                variants.append(var_token.lexeme)
 666        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '}' after enum variants")
 667        return EnumDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, variants=variants, span=self._span(start))
 668
 669    def _parse_struct_decl(self) -> StructDecl:
 670        start = self._advance()
 671        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 672                                  detail="Expected struct name")
 673        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for struct fields")
 674        fields = []
 675        while not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self._is_at_end():
 676            field_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 677                                       detail="Expected field name")
 678            field_type = None
 679            if self._match(TokenType.COLON):
 680                type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 681                                          detail="Expected field type")
 682                field_type = type_token.lexeme
 683            fields.append((field_token.lexeme, field_type))
 684            self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 685        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '}' after struct fields")
 686        return StructDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, fields=fields, span=self._span(start))
 687
 688    def _parse_interface_decl(self) -> InterfaceDecl:
 689        start = self._advance()
 690        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 691                                  detail="Expected interface name")
 692        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for interface methods")
 693        methods = []
 694        while not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self._is_at_end():
 695            method_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 696                                        detail="Expected method name")
 697            params = self._parse_params()
 698            ret_type = None
 699            if self._match(TokenType.ARROW):
 700                type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 701                                          detail="Expected return type")
 702                ret_type = type_token.lexeme
 703            methods.append((method_token.lexeme, params, ret_type))
 704            self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 705        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '}' after interface methods")
 706        return InterfaceDecl(name=name_token.lexeme, methods=methods, span=self._span(start))
 707
 708    def _parse_cascade_call(self) -> CascadeCall:
 709        start = self._advance()
 710        expr = self._parse_expr()
 711        return CascadeCall(expr=expr, span=self._span(start))
 712
 713    def _parse_new_expr(self) -> NewExpr:
 714        start = self._advance()
 715        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 716                                  detail="Expected class name after 'new'")
 717        args = []
 718        if self._match(TokenType.LPAREN):
 719            if not self._check(TokenType.RPAREN):
 720                args.append(self._parse_expr())
 721                while self._match(TokenType.COMMA):
 722                    args.append(self._parse_expr())
 723            self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after arguments")
 724        return NewExpr(class_name=name_token.lexeme, args=args, span=self._span(start))
 725
 726    def _parse_shadow_thirst_mutation(self) -> ShadowThirstMutation:
 727        start = self._advance()
 728        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 729                                  detail="Expected mutation name")
 730        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for mutation body")
 731        self._expect(TokenType.VALIDATED_CANONICAL, "E901",
 732                      detail="Expected 'validated_canonical'")
 733        self._expect(TokenType.LBRACE, "E901", detail="Expected '{' for validated_canonical")
 734        shadow_block = None
 735        invariant_block = None
 736        canonical_block = None
 737        while not self._check(TokenType.RBRACE) and not self._is_at_end():
 738            if self._match(TokenType.SHADOW):
 739                shadow_block = self._parse_block()
 740            elif self._match(TokenType.INVARIANT):
 741                invariant_block = self._parse_block()
 742            elif self._match(TokenType.CANONICAL):
 743                canonical_block = self._parse_block()
 744            else:
 745                self._advance()
 746        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901",
 747                      detail="Expected '}' after validated_canonical")
 748        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACE, "E901",
 749                      detail="Expected '}' after mutation body")
 750        return ShadowThirstMutation(name=name_token.lexeme,
 751                                    shadow_block=shadow_block,
 752                                    invariant_block=invariant_block,
 753                                    canonical_block=canonical_block,
 754                                    span=self._span(start))
 755
 756    def _parse_tscg_symbol(self) -> object:
 757        start = self._advance()
 758        name_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 759                                  detail="Expected TSCG symbol name")
 760        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 761        return SymbolStmt(symbol_name=name_token.lexeme, span=self._span(start))
 762
 763    def _parse_expr_statement(self) -> Stmt:
 764        start = self._peek()
 765        expr = self._parse_expr()
 766        self._match(TokenType.SEMICOLON)
 767        # `x = y` parses as an AssignStmt; return it directly so it dispatches
 768        # to _execute_assign. Wrapping it in an ExprStmt would route it through
 769        # expression evaluation, which has no AssignStmt branch — making the
 770        # assignment a silent no-op.
 771        if isinstance(expr, AssignStmt):
 772            return expr
 773        return ExprStmt(expr=expr, span=self._span(start))
 774
 775    # === Expression Parsing with Precedence ===
 776
 777    def _parse_expr(self, precedence: int = 0) -> Expr:
 778        """Parse expression with Pratt-style precedence climbing."""
 779        prefix = self._parse_prefix()
 780        if prefix is None:
 781            t = self._peek()
 782            msg = f"Unexpected token: {t.type.name} ('{t.lexeme}')"
 783            # Check "did you mean?" for identifiers
 784            if t.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
 785                suggestions = _nearest_match(t.lexeme, list(KEYWORDS.keys()))
 786                if suggestions:
 787                    msg += f" Did you mean: {', '.join(suggestions)}?"
 788            self.errors.append(make_error("E901", span=self._current_span(), detail=msg))
 789            self._advance()
 790            return Identifier(name="__error__", span=self._current_span())
 791
 792        while precedence < self._get_precedence():
 793            token = self._peek()
 794            op = token.type
 795            # Capture the operator's precedence BEFORE advancing past it. After
 796            # _advance() the current token is the right operand (precedence 0 for
 797            # literals/identifiers), so reading precedence post-advance silently
 798            # collapsed every operator to one right-associative level.
 799            op_prec = self._get_precedence()
 800            # The loop guard above is strict (`precedence < peek`), so a left-
 801            # associative operator recurses at `op_prec` (the right side grabs
 802            # only strictly-tighter operators; an equal-precedence operator
 803            # returns here and binds to the new left). A right-associative
 804            # operator recurses at `op_prec - 1` so it also grabs its equal.
 805            if op == TokenType.ASSIGN or op == TokenType.EQ:
 806                self._advance()
 807                right = self._parse_expr(op_prec - 1)  # right-associative
 808                # Assignment is parsed at expression precedence but yields a
 809                # statement node (AssignStmt); the caller handles it as such.
 810                return AssignStmt(  # type: ignore[return-value]
 811                    target=prefix, value=right,
 812                    span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 813                          right.span[2], right.span[3]))
 814            elif op == TokenType.LPAREN:
 815                prefix = self._parse_call_suffix(prefix)
 816            elif op == TokenType.PIPE:
 817                self._advance()
 818                right = self._parse_expr(op_prec)
 819                prefix = PipeExpr(left=prefix, right=right,
 820                                  span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 821                                        right.span[2], right.span[3]))
 822            elif op in (TokenType.PLUS, TokenType.MINUS, TokenType.STAR,
 823                        TokenType.SLASH, TokenType.PERCENT, TokenType.EQEQ,
 824                        TokenType.NE, TokenType.LT, TokenType.GT,
 825                        TokenType.LE, TokenType.GE, TokenType.AND,
 826                        TokenType.OR):
 827                self._advance()
 828                right = self._parse_expr(op_prec)  # left-associative
 829                prefix = BinaryOp(left=prefix, op=op, right=right,
 830                                  span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 831                                        right.span[2], right.span[3]))
 832            elif op == TokenType.PIPEPIPE:
 833                self._advance()
 834                right = self._parse_expr(op_prec)
 835                prefix = CombineExpr(left=prefix, op="||", right=right,
 836                                     span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 837                                           right.span[2], right.span[3]))
 838            elif op == TokenType.HATHAT:
 839                self._advance()
 840                right = self._parse_expr(op_prec)
 841                prefix = CombineExpr(left=prefix, op="^", right=right,
 842                                     span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 843                                           right.span[2], right.span[3]))
 844            elif op == TokenType.ARROW:
 845                self._advance()
 846                right = self._parse_expr(op_prec)
 847                prefix = PipelineExpr(left=prefix, right=right,
 848                                      span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 849                                            right.span[2], right.span[3]))
 850            elif op == TokenType.DOT:
 851                self._advance()
 852                # After '.', the word is a member name in the object's
 853                # namespace, not a language keyword: accept any token whose
 854                # lexeme is a valid identifier (e.g. `log.error`, where `error`
 855                # otherwise lexes as the ERROR keyword).
 856                if (not self._check(TokenType.IDENTIFIER)
 857                        and self._peek().lexeme.isidentifier()):
 858                    member_token = self._advance()
 859                else:
 860                    member_token = self._expect(
 861                        TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 862                        detail="Expected method/property name after '.'")
 863                member = MemberAccess(
 864                    obj=prefix, member=member_token.lexeme,
 865                    span=(prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 866                          member_token.line,
 867                          member_token.col + len(member_token.lexeme)))
 868                if self._check(TokenType.LPAREN):
 869                    # method call: obj.method(args) — object carried by callee
 870                    args = []
 871                    self._advance()
 872                    if not self._check(TokenType.RPAREN):
 873                        args.append(self._parse_expr())
 874                        while self._match(TokenType.COMMA):
 875                            args.append(self._parse_expr())
 876                    self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901",
 877                                 detail="Expected ')' after arguments")
 878                    span = (prefix.span[0], prefix.span[1],
 879                            self._previous().line, self._previous().col + 1)
 880                    prefix = CallExpr(callee=member, args=args, span=span)
 881                else:
 882                    prefix = member
 883            else:
 884                break
 885        return prefix
 886
 887    def _parse_prefix(self) -> Expr | None:
 888        """Parse prefix expressions (literals, identifiers, unary operators)."""
 889        t = self._peek().type
 890
 891        if t == TokenType.INT:
 892            return self._parse_int_literal()
 893        elif t == TokenType.FLOAT:
 894            return self._parse_float_literal()
 895        elif t == TokenType.STRING:
 896            return self._parse_string_literal()
 897        elif t == TokenType.BOOL_TRUE:
 898            return self._parse_bool_literal(True)
 899        elif t == TokenType.BOOL_FALSE:
 900            return self._parse_bool_literal(False)
 901        elif t == TokenType.NONE:
 902            return self._parse_none_literal()
 903        elif t == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
 904            id_token = self._advance()
 905            return Identifier(name=id_token.lexeme, span=self._span(id_token))
 906        elif t == TokenType.LPAREN:
 907            self._advance()
 908            expr = self._parse_expr()
 909            self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after expression")
 910            return expr
 911        elif t == TokenType.LBRACKET:
 912            return self._parse_reservoir_literal()
 913        elif t == TokenType.MINUS:
 914            self._advance()
 915            # Unary binds tighter than every binary operator: parse only the
 916            # primary (with member/call suffixes), not a trailing binary chain,
 917            # so -2 + 3 is (-2) + 3, not -(2 + 3).
 918            operand = self._parse_expr(self.UNARY_PRECEDENCE)
 919            return UnaryOp(operand=operand, op=TokenType.MINUS,
 920                           span=self._span())
 921        elif t == TokenType.NOT:
 922            self._advance()
 923            # Logical `not` binds looser than comparison but tighter than
 924            # `and`/`or`: not a == b is not (a == b); not a and b is
 925            # (not a) and b. Operand grabs everything above the `and` level.
 926            operand = self._parse_expr(self.NOT_PRECEDENCE)
 927            return UnaryOp(operand=operand, op=TokenType.NOT,
 928                           span=self._span())
 929        elif t == TokenType.FLOOD:
 930            self._advance()
 931            target = self._parse_expr()
 932            return FloodExpr(target=target, span=self._span())
 933        elif t == TokenType.DRIP:
 934            self._advance()
 935            target = self._parse_expr()
 936            return DripExpr(target=target, span=self._span())
 937        elif t == TokenType.CONDENSE:
 938            self._advance()
 939            target = self._parse_expr()
 940            return CondenseExpr(target=target, span=self._span())
 941        elif t == TokenType.EVAPORATE:
 942            self._advance()
 943            target = self._parse_expr()
 944            return EvaporateExpr(target=target, span=self._span())
 945        elif t == TokenType.THIS:
 946            # `this` is the current fountain instance. Model it as an identifier
 947            # named "this"; member access/assignment and method calls are then
 948            # handled by the existing DOT/ASSIGN infix machinery, and the
 949            # interpreter binds "this" in each method's call scope.
 950            this_token = self._advance()
 951            return Identifier(name="this", span=self._span(this_token))
 952        elif t == TokenType.THIRST:
 953            return self._parse_guard_expr()
 954        elif t == TokenType.QUENCHED:
 955            return self._parse_quenched_literal()
 956        elif t == TokenType.SANITIZE:
 957            return self._parse_sanitize_expr()
 958        elif t == TokenType.ARMOR:
 959            return self._parse_armor_expr()
 960        elif t == TokenType.NEW:
 961            return self._parse_new_expr()
 962        elif t == TokenType.CASCADE:
 963            return self._parse_cascade_call()
 964        elif t == TokenType.GLASS:
 965            return self._parse_lambda_expr()
 966        return None
 967
 968    def _parse_lambda_expr(self) -> LambdaExpr:
 969        """glass(params) [-> type] { body } — anonymous function expression."""
 970        start = self._advance()  # glass
 971        params = self._parse_params()
 972        return_type = None
 973        if self._match(TokenType.ARROW):
 974            type_token = self._expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, "E901",
 975                                      detail="Expected return type after '->'")
 976            return_type = type_token.lexeme
 977        body = self._parse_block()
 978        return LambdaExpr(params=params, body=body,
 979                          return_type=return_type, span=self._span(start))
 980
 981    def _parse_int_literal(self) -> IntLiteral:
 982        t = self._advance()
 983        value = int(t.lexeme, 0) if t.lexeme.startswith(("0x", "0X", "0b", "0B", "0o", "0O")) else int(t.lexeme)
 984        return IntLiteral(value=value, span=self._span(t))
 985
 986    def _parse_float_literal(self) -> FloatLiteral:
 987        t = self._advance()
 988        return FloatLiteral(value=float(t.lexeme), span=self._span(t))
 989
 990    def _parse_string_literal(self) -> StringLiteral:
 991        t = self._advance()
 992        return StringLiteral(value=t.lexeme, span=self._span(t))
 993
 994    def _parse_bool_literal(self, value: bool) -> BoolLiteral:
 995        t = self._advance()
 996        return BoolLiteral(value=value, span=self._span(t))
 997
 998    def _parse_none_literal(self) -> NoneLiteral:
 999        t = self._advance()
1000        return NoneLiteral(span=self._span(t))
1001
1002    def _parse_quenched_literal(self) -> QuenchedLiteral:
1003        t = self._advance()
1004        type_param = "Any"
1005        value = None
1006        if self._match(TokenType.LPAREN):
1007            if not self._check(TokenType.RPAREN):
1008                value = self._parse_expr()
1009            self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after quenched value")
1010        return QuenchedLiteral(type_param=type_param, value=value, span=self._span(t))
1011
1012    def _parse_reservoir_literal(self) -> Expr:
1013        """Parse [elem1, elem2, ...] reservoir literal."""
1014        start = self._advance()
1015        elements = []
1016        if not self._check(TokenType.RBRACKET):
1017            elements.append(self._parse_expr())
1018            while self._match(TokenType.COMMA):
1019                elements.append(self._parse_expr())
1020        self._expect(TokenType.RBRACKET, "E901", detail="Expected ']' after reservoir literal")
1021        return ArrayLiteral(elements=elements, span=self._span(start))
1022
1023    def _parse_call_suffix(self, callee: Expr) -> Expr:
1024        self._advance()  # consume (
1025        args = []
1026        if not self._check(TokenType.RPAREN):
1027            args.append(self._parse_expr())
1028            while self._match(TokenType.COMMA):
1029                args.append(self._parse_expr())
1030        self._expect(TokenType.RPAREN, "E901", detail="Expected ')' after arguments")
1031        return CallExpr(callee=callee, args=args, span=(callee.span[0], callee.span[1],
1032                                                         self._previous().line,
1033                                                         self._previous().col + 1))
1034
1035    def _parse_guard_expr(self) -> GuardExpr:
1036        start = self._advance()  # thirst
1037        expr = self._parse_expr()
1038        self._expect(TokenType.QUENCH, "E901", detail="Expected 'quench' after guard expression")
1039        condition = self._parse_expr()
1040        return GuardExpr(expr=expr, condition=condition, span=self._span(start))
1041
1042    # === Precedence Table ===
1043
1044    # Binding power for prefix unary negation (-). Above multiplicative (7) so
1045    # the operand is just the primary plus its member/call suffixes (DOT/LPAREN
1046    # are 9, still tighter), never a trailing binary chain: -2 * 3 is (-2) * 3.
1047    UNARY_PRECEDENCE = 8
1048    # Binding power for logical `not`. At the `and` level (4) so the operand
1049    # grabs comparisons/arithmetic (>= 5) but not `and`/`or`.
1050    NOT_PRECEDENCE = 4
1051
1052    def _get_precedence(self) -> int:
1053        """Return precedence of the current token (higher = binds tighter)."""
1054        token = self._peek()
1055        return self._precedence_map().get(token.type, 0)
1056
1057    def _precedence_map(self) -> dict[TokenType, int]:
1058        return {
1059            TokenType.ASSIGN: 1,
1060            TokenType.EQ: 1,
1061            TokenType.PIPE: 2,
1062            TokenType.THIRST: 2,
1063            TokenType.OR: 3,
1064            TokenType.AND: 4,
1065            TokenType.EQEQ: 5,
1066            TokenType.NE: 5,
1067            TokenType.LT: 5,
1068            TokenType.GT: 5,
1069            TokenType.LE: 5,
1070            TokenType.GE: 5,
1071            TokenType.PLUS: 6,
1072            TokenType.MINUS: 6,
1073            TokenType.STAR: 7,
1074            TokenType.SLASH: 7,
1075            TokenType.PERCENT: 7,
1076            TokenType.ARROW: 8,
1077            TokenType.PIPEPIPE: 8,
1078            TokenType.HATHAT: 8,
1079            TokenType.DOT: 9,
1080            TokenType.LPAREN: 9,
1081        }
1082
1083    def _peek_next(self, n: int = 1) -> Token:
1084        """Look ahead n tokens without consuming."""
1085        idx = self.current + n
1086        if idx >= len(self.tokens):
1087            return self.tokens[-1]
1088        return self.tokens[idx]

Recursive descent parser for Thirsty-Lang.

Parser(tokens: list[Token]) 
92    def __init__(self, tokens: list[Token]):
93        self.tokens = tokens
94        self.current = 0
95        self.errors: list[Diagnostic] = []
tokens
current
errors: list[Diagnostic]
def parse(self) -> Program: 
 97    def parse(self) -> Program:
 98        """Parse the token stream into a Program AST."""
 99        header = self._parse_module_header()
100        stmts = []
101        while not self._is_at_end():
102            try:
103                stmt = self._parse_statement()
104                if stmt is not None:
105                    stmts.append(stmt)
106                else:
107                    self._advance()  # Skip unrecognized
108            except Exception as e:
109                self.errors.append(make_error("E901", span=self._current_span(),
110                                              detail=str(e)))
111                self._synchronize()
112        span = self._span_range()
113        # Fail closed for governed modules: if parsing produced any errors, the
114        # recovered statements cannot be trusted to preserve author intent, so
115        # discard them entirely rather than letting recovery smuggle executable
116        # statements past a malformed boundary. The interpreter refuses to run a
117        # program flagged this way.
118        if self.errors and header is not None and header.mode == "governed":
119            return Program(stmts=[], header=header, span=span,
120                           parse_failed=True)
121        return Program(stmts=stmts, header=header, span=span)

Parse the token stream into a Program AST.

UNARY_PRECEDENCE = 8
NOT_PRECEDENCE = 4
class Type: 
 9class Type:
10    """Base class for all types."""
11    def __str__(self):
12        return type_to_string(self)

Base class for all types.

@dataclass

17    pass

class FloatType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type):

21class FloatType(Type):
22    pass

class BoolType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type):

26class BoolType(Type):
27    pass
@dataclass
class StringType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
30@dataclass
31class StringType(Type):
32    pass
@dataclass
class VoidType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
35@dataclass
36class VoidType(Type):
37    pass
@dataclass
class AnyType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
40@dataclass
41class AnyType(Type):
42    pass
@dataclass
class ErrorType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
45@dataclass
46class ErrorType(Type):
47    message: str = ""
ErrorType(message: str = '')
message: str = ''
@dataclass
class GenericType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
52@dataclass
53class GenericType(Type):
54    base_type: str  # "Quenched", "Reservoir", "Task", "Governed"
55    type_args: list[Type] = field(default_factory=list)
GenericType( base_type: str, type_args: list[Type] = <factory>)
base_type: str
type_args: list[Type]
class QuenchedType(utf.thirsty_lang.GenericType): 
58class QuenchedType(GenericType):
59    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
60        super().__init__(base_type="Quenched", type_args=[type_arg])
61
62    @property
63    def inner_type(self) -> Type:
64        return self.type_args[0] if self.type_args else AnyType()

GenericType(base_type: str, type_args: list[utf.thirsty_lang.typesys.Type] = )

QuenchedType(type_arg: Type) 
59    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
60        super().__init__(base_type="Quenched", type_args=[type_arg])
inner_type: Type 
62    @property
63    def inner_type(self) -> Type:
64        return self.type_args[0] if self.type_args else AnyType()
class ReservoirType(utf.thirsty_lang.GenericType): 
67class ReservoirType(GenericType):
68    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
69        super().__init__(base_type="Reservoir", type_args=[type_arg])
70
71    @property
72    def inner_type(self) -> Type:
73        return self.type_args[0] if self.type_args else AnyType()

GenericType(base_type: str, type_args: list[utf.thirsty_lang.typesys.Type] = )

ReservoirType(type_arg: Type) 
68    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
69        super().__init__(base_type="Reservoir", type_args=[type_arg])
inner_type: Type 
71    @property
72    def inner_type(self) -> Type:
73        return self.type_args[0] if self.type_args else AnyType()
class TaskType(utf.thirsty_lang.GenericType): 
76class TaskType(GenericType):
77    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
78        super().__init__(base_type="Task", type_args=[type_arg])

GenericType(base_type: str, type_args: list[utf.thirsty_lang.typesys.Type] = )

TaskType(type_arg: Type) 
77    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
78        super().__init__(base_type="Task", type_args=[type_arg])
class ResultType(utf.thirsty_lang.GenericType): 
81class ResultType(GenericType):
82    def __init__(self, ok_type: Type, err_type: Type):
83        super().__init__(base_type="Result", type_args=[ok_type, err_type])
84
85    @property
86    def ok_type(self) -> Type:
87        return self.type_args[0] if len(self.type_args) > 0 else AnyType()
88
89    @property
90    def err_type(self) -> Type:
91        return self.type_args[1] if len(self.type_args) > 1 else AnyType()

GenericType(base_type: str, type_args: list[utf.thirsty_lang.typesys.Type] = )

ResultType( ok_type: Type, err_type: Type) 
82    def __init__(self, ok_type: Type, err_type: Type):
83        super().__init__(base_type="Result", type_args=[ok_type, err_type])
ok_type: Type 
85    @property
86    def ok_type(self) -> Type:
87        return self.type_args[0] if len(self.type_args) > 0 else AnyType()
err_type: Type 
89    @property
90    def err_type(self) -> Type:
91        return self.type_args[1] if len(self.type_args) > 1 else AnyType()
class GovernedType(utf.thirsty_lang.GenericType):

95    def __init__(self, type_arg: Type):
96        super().__init__(base_type="Governed", type_args=[type_arg])

GenericType(base_type: str, type_args: list[utf.thirsty_lang.typesys.Type] = )

GovernedType(type_arg: Type)

96        super().__init__(base_type="Governed", type_args=[type_arg])
@dataclass

102class EnumType(Type):

104    variants: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
EnumType(name: str, variants: list[str] = <factory>)

variants: list[str]
@dataclass
class StructType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type):

108class StructType(Type):
109    name: str
110    field_types: dict[str, Type] = field(default_factory=dict)
StructType( name: str, field_types: dict[str, Type] = <factory>)
name: str
field_types: dict[str, Type]
@dataclass
class InterfaceType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type):

114class InterfaceType(Type):
115    name: str
116    method_sigs: dict[str, list[Type]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # name -> [param_types]
InterfaceType( name: str, method_sigs: dict[str, list[Type]] = <factory>)
name: str
method_sigs: dict[str, list[Type]]
@dataclass
class TypeVariable(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
121@dataclass
122class TypeVariable(Type):
123    name: str  # e.g., "T", "E"
TypeVariable(name: str)
name: str
@dataclass
class FunctionType(utf.thirsty_lang.Type): 
128@dataclass
129class FunctionType(Type):
130    param_types: list[Type] = field(default_factory=list)
131    return_type: Type = field(default_factory=VoidType)
FunctionType( param_types: list[Type] = <factory>, return_type: Type = <factory>)
param_types: list[Type]
return_type: Type
def is_assignable( source: Type, target: Type) -> bool: 
236def is_assignable(source: Type, target: Type) -> bool:
237    """Check if a value of source type can be assigned to a target type variable."""
238    if isinstance(target, AnyType):
239        return True
240    if isinstance(source, AnyType):
241        return True
242    if type(source) is type(target):
243        if isinstance(source, QuenchedType) and isinstance(target, QuenchedType):
244            return is_assignable(source.inner_type, target.inner_type)
245        if isinstance(source, ReservoirType) and isinstance(target, ReservoirType):
246            return is_assignable(source.inner_type, target.inner_type)
247        if isinstance(source, FunctionType) and isinstance(target, FunctionType):
248            if len(source.param_types) != len(target.param_types):
249                return False
250            for sp, tp in zip(source.param_types, target.param_types, strict=False):
251                if not is_assignable(sp, tp):
252                    return False
253            return is_assignable(source.return_type, target.return_type)
254        return True  # same type
255    # Int -> Float widening
256    if isinstance(source, IntType) and isinstance(target, FloatType):
257        return True
258    # Different type constructors are not assignable. (Same-named enums/structs
259    # are already accepted above by the same-class check.)
260    return False

Check if a value of source type can be assigned to a target type variable.

def type_to_string(t: Type) -> str: 
263def type_to_string(t: Type) -> str:
264    """Convert a Type object to its string representation."""
265    if isinstance(t, IntType):
266        return "Int"
267    elif isinstance(t, FloatType):
268        return "Float"
269    elif isinstance(t, BoolType):
270        return "Bool"
271    elif isinstance(t, StringType):
272        return "String"
273    elif isinstance(t, VoidType):
274        return "Void"
275    elif isinstance(t, AnyType):
276        return "Any"
277    elif isinstance(t, ErrorType):
278        return "Error"
279    elif isinstance(t, QuenchedType):
280        return f"Quenched[{type_to_string(t.inner_type)}]"
281    elif isinstance(t, ReservoirType):
282        return f"Reservoir[{type_to_string(t.inner_type)}]"
283    elif isinstance(t, TaskType):
284        inner = type_to_string(t.type_args[0]) if t.type_args else "Any"
285        return f"Task[{inner}]"
286    elif isinstance(t, ResultType):
287        ok = type_to_string(t.ok_type)
288        err = type_to_string(t.err_type)
289        return f"Result[{ok}, {err}]"
290    elif isinstance(t, GovernedType):
291        inner = type_to_string(t.type_args[0]) if t.type_args else "Any"
292        return f"Governed[{inner}]"
293    elif isinstance(t, EnumType):
294        return t.name
295    elif isinstance(t, StructType):
296        return t.name
297    elif isinstance(t, InterfaceType):
298        return t.name
299    elif isinstance(t, TypeVariable):
300        return t.name
301    elif isinstance(t, FunctionType):
302        params = ", ".join(type_to_string(p) for p in t.param_types)
303        ret = type_to_string(t.return_type)
304        return f"({params}) -> {ret}"
305    elif isinstance(t, GenericType):
306        args = ", ".join(type_to_string(a) for a in t.type_args)
307        return f"{t.base_type}[{args}]"
308    else:
309        return str(t)

Convert a Type object to its string representation.

def unify( t1: Type, t2: Type) -> Type: 
209def unify(t1: Type, t2: Type) -> Type:
210    """Find the most specific common supertype of t1 and t2."""
211    if isinstance(t1, AnyType) or isinstance(t2, AnyType):
212        return AnyType()
213    if type(t1) is type(t2):
214        # Same base type — check generics
215        if isinstance(t1, QuenchedType) and isinstance(t2, QuenchedType):
216            inner = unify(t1.inner_type, t2.inner_type)
217            return QuenchedType(inner)
218        if isinstance(t1, ReservoirType) and isinstance(t2, ReservoirType):
219            inner = unify(t1.inner_type, t2.inner_type)
220            return ReservoirType(inner)
221        if isinstance(t1, FunctionType) and isinstance(t2, FunctionType):
222            if len(t1.param_types) == len(t2.param_types):
223                params = [unify(p1, p2) for p1, p2 in zip(t1.param_types, t2.param_types, strict=False)]
224                ret = unify(t1.return_type, t2.return_type)
225                return FunctionType(params, ret)
226        return t1
227    # Widening
228    if isinstance(t1, IntType) and isinstance(t2, FloatType):
229        return FloatType()
230    if isinstance(t1, FloatType) and isinstance(t2, IntType):
231        return FloatType()
232    # Fall through to Any
233    return AnyType()

Find the most specific common supertype of t1 and t2.

class Checker: 
136class Checker:
137    """Semantic analysis and type checking for Thirsty-Lang AST."""
138
139    def __init__(self):
140        self.scope = Scope()
141        self.errors: list[Diagnostic] = []
142        self.current_function_return_type: Type | None = None
143        self.in_governed_mode = False
144        self.imported_names: dict[str, str] = {}  # alias -> module_path
145        self._hoisted: set = set()         # top-level fn/class names
146        self._governed_names: set = set()  # governed function names
147
148    def enter_scope(self):
149        self.scope = Scope(self.scope)
150
151    def exit_scope(self):
152        if self.scope.parent:
153            self.scope = self.scope.parent
154
155    def check_all(self, ast: Program) -> list[Diagnostic]:
156        """Type-check an entire Program AST."""
157        self.errors = []
158        # Register mode
159        if ast.header:
160            self.in_governed_mode = (ast.header.mode == "governed")
161            # Register module name
162            self.scope.declare("__module__", {"type": StringType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "module"})
163        # Register builtins
164        self._register_builtins()
165        # Hoist top-level function/class names so forward references resolve.
166        self._hoist_declarations(ast.stmts)
167        # Check all statements
168        for stmt in ast.stmts:
169            self._check_stmt(stmt)
170        return self.errors
171
172    def _hoist_declarations(self, stmts):
173        """Pre-declare top-level functions/classes (enables forward references
174        and mutual recursion) and record which functions are governed."""
175        self._hoisted = set()
176        self._governed_names = set()
177        for stmt in stmts:
178            name = getattr(stmt, "name", None)
179            if isinstance(stmt, (FunctionDecl, GovernedFunctionDecl)):
180                if name in self._hoisted:
181                    self.errors.append(
182                        make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=name))
183                    continue
184                # Register the real signature (param + return types) so that
185                # forward references, self-recursion, and mutual recursion all
186                # see the correct arity while bodies are being checked.
187                param_types = [type_from_name(ptype) if ptype else AnyType()
188                               for _pname, ptype in stmt.params]
189                ret_type = (type_from_name(stmt.return_type)
190                            if stmt.return_type else VoidType())
191                self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {
192                    "type": FunctionType(param_types, ret_type),
193                    "is_mut": False, "kind": "function"})
194                self._hoisted.add(name)
195                if isinstance(stmt, GovernedFunctionDecl):
196                    self._governed_names.add(name)
197            elif isinstance(stmt, ClassDecl):
198                if name in self._hoisted:
199                    self.errors.append(
200                        make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=name))
201                    continue
202                self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {
203                    "type": AnyType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "class"})
204                self._hoisted.add(name)
205
206    def _register_builtins(self):
207        builtins = [
208            "length", "contains", "split", "abs", "min", "max",
209            "push", "pop", "size", "get", "flood",
210            "condense", "evaporate", "strain", "transmute", "distill",
211            "pour", "sip", "print",
212        ]
213        for name in builtins:
214            self.scope.declare(name, {"type": AnyType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "builtin"})
215
216    def _check_stmt(self, stmt: Stmt):
217        if isinstance(stmt, VariableDecl):
218            self._check_variable_decl(stmt)
219        elif isinstance(stmt, FunctionDecl):
220            self._check_function_decl(stmt)
221        elif isinstance(stmt, ClassDecl):
222            self._check_class_decl(stmt)
223        elif isinstance(stmt, IfStmt):
224            self._check_if_stmt(stmt)
225        elif isinstance(stmt, WhileStmt):
226            self._check_while_stmt(stmt)
227        elif isinstance(stmt, ForStmt):
228            self._check_for_stmt(stmt)
229        elif isinstance(stmt, TimesStmt):
230            self._check_times_stmt(stmt)
231        elif isinstance(stmt, BlockStmt):
232            self._check_block(stmt)
233        elif isinstance(stmt, ReturnStmt):
234            self._check_return_stmt(stmt)
235        elif isinstance(stmt, PourStmt):
236            self._check_expr(stmt.value)
237        elif isinstance(stmt, SipStmt):
238            self._check_expr(stmt.target)
239        elif isinstance(stmt, AssignStmt):
240            self._check_assign_stmt(stmt)
241        elif isinstance(stmt, SymbolStmt):
242            self._check_symbol_stmt(stmt)
243        elif isinstance(stmt, ImportStmt):
244            self._check_import_stmt(stmt)
245        elif isinstance(stmt, ExprStmt):
246            self._check_expr(stmt.expr)
247        elif isinstance(stmt, SecurityBlock):
248            self._check_block(stmt.body)
249        elif isinstance(stmt, SpillageStmt):
250            self._check_block(stmt.body)
251            for error_var, handler in stmt.handlers:
252                self.enter_scope()
253                if error_var:
254                    self.scope.declare(
255                        error_var,
256                        {"type": AnyType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "error"})
257                self._check_block(handler)
258                self.exit_scope()
259        elif isinstance(stmt, CleanupStmt):
260            self._check_block(stmt.body)
261            self._check_block(stmt.finalizer)
262        elif isinstance(stmt, ThrowStmt):
263            self._check_expr(stmt.value)
264        elif isinstance(stmt, MorphDef):
265            self._check_block(stmt.body)
266        elif isinstance(stmt, DefendStrat):
267            pass  # policy names are validated at runtime
268        elif isinstance(stmt, EnumDecl):
269            self._check_enum_decl(stmt)
270        elif isinstance(stmt, StructDecl):
271            self._check_struct_decl(stmt)
272        elif isinstance(stmt, InterfaceDecl):
273            self._check_interface_decl(stmt)
274        elif isinstance(stmt, GovernedFunctionDecl):
275            self._check_governed_function_decl(stmt)
276        elif isinstance(stmt, ShadowThirstMutation):
277            # Validate mutation blocks exist
278            pass
279
280    def _check_variable_decl(self, stmt: VariableDecl):
281        # Check duplicate
282        if not self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {"type": None, "is_mut": stmt.is_mut, "kind": "variable"}):
283            self.errors.append(make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.name))
284            return
285        # Check init expression
286        if stmt.init_expr:
287            expr_type = self._check_expr(stmt.init_expr)
288            if stmt.var_type:
289                expected = type_from_name(stmt.var_type)
290                if not is_assignable(expr_type, expected):
291                    self.errors.append(make_error("E021", span=stmt.span,
292                                                  found=type_to_string(expr_type),
293                                                  expected=type_to_string(expected)))
294            # Update binding type
295            self.scope.bindings[stmt.name] = {
296                "type": expr_type if not stmt.var_type else type_from_name(stmt.var_type),
297                "is_mut": stmt.is_mut,
298                "kind": "variable"
299            }
300
301    def _check_function_decl(self, stmt: FunctionDecl):
302        # Top-level names are pre-declared by the hoist pass; only declare (and
303        # duplicate-check) names that weren't hoisted (e.g. class methods).
304        already_hoisted = (stmt.name in self._hoisted
305                           and self.scope.bindings.get(stmt.name) is not None)
306        if not already_hoisted:
307            if not self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {"type": FunctionType([], AnyType()), "is_mut": False, "kind": "function"}):
308                self.errors.append(make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.name))
309                return
310        self.enter_scope()
311        # Register params
312        param_types = []
313        for pname, ptype in stmt.params:
314            t = type_from_name(ptype) if ptype else AnyType()
315            param_types.append(t)
316            self.scope.declare(pname, {"type": t, "is_mut": False, "kind": "param"})
317        # Set return type context
318        old_return_type = self.current_function_return_type
319        self.current_function_return_type = type_from_name(stmt.return_type) if stmt.return_type else VoidType()
320        # Check body
321        self._check_block(stmt.body)
322        self.current_function_return_type = old_return_type
323        self.exit_scope()
324        # Update binding with proper function type
325        ret_type = type_from_name(stmt.return_type) if stmt.return_type else VoidType()
326        self.scope.bindings[stmt.name] = {
327            "type": FunctionType(param_types, ret_type),
328            "is_mut": False,
329            "kind": "function"
330        }
331
332    def _check_class_decl(self, stmt: ClassDecl):
333        already_hoisted = (stmt.name in self._hoisted
334                           and self.scope.bindings.get(stmt.name) is not None)
335        if not already_hoisted:
336            if not self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {"type": AnyType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "class"}):
337                self.errors.append(make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.name))
338        self.enter_scope()
339        for field_spec in stmt.fields:
340            fname, ftype = field_spec[0], field_spec[1]
341            t = type_from_name(ftype) if ftype else AnyType()
342            self.scope.declare(fname, {"type": t, "is_mut": True, "kind": "field"})
343            default_expr = field_spec[2] if len(field_spec) > 2 else None
344            if default_expr is not None:
345                self._check_expr(default_expr)
346        for method in stmt.methods:
347            self._check_function_decl(method)
348        self.exit_scope()
349
350    def _check_if_stmt(self, stmt: IfStmt):
351        cond_type = self._check_expr(stmt.condition)
352        if not isinstance(cond_type, BoolType) and not isinstance(cond_type, AnyType):
353            self.errors.append(make_error("E024", span=stmt.condition.span,
354                                          found=type_to_string(cond_type)))
355        self._check_block(stmt.then_block)
356        if stmt.else_block:
357            if isinstance(stmt.else_block, IfStmt):
358                self._check_if_stmt(stmt.else_block)
359            else:
360                self._check_block(stmt.else_block)
361
362    def _check_while_stmt(self, stmt: WhileStmt):
363        cond_type = self._check_expr(stmt.condition)
364        if not isinstance(cond_type, BoolType) and not isinstance(cond_type, AnyType):
365            self.errors.append(make_error("E024", span=stmt.condition.span,
366                                          found=type_to_string(cond_type)))
367        self._check_block(stmt.body)
368
369    def _check_for_stmt(self, stmt: ForStmt):
370        self.enter_scope()
371        self.scope.declare(stmt.variable.name, {"type": AnyType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "loop_var"})
372        self._check_expr(stmt.iterable)
373        self._check_block(stmt.body)
374        self.exit_scope()
375
376    def _check_times_stmt(self, stmt: TimesStmt):
377        # The count is evaluated to an int at runtime; just resolve the
378        # expression (catches unknown identifiers) and check the body.
379        self._check_expr(stmt.count)
380        self._check_block(stmt.body)
381
382    def _check_block(self, stmt: Stmt):
383        if isinstance(stmt, BlockStmt):
384            self.enter_scope()
385            for s in stmt.statements:
386                self._check_stmt(s)
387            self.exit_scope()
388        else:
389            self._check_stmt(stmt)
390
391    def _check_return_stmt(self, stmt: ReturnStmt):
392        if stmt.value:
393            val_type = self._check_expr(stmt.value)
394            if self.current_function_return_type and not isinstance(self.current_function_return_type, VoidType):
395                if not is_assignable(val_type, self.current_function_return_type):
396                    self.errors.append(make_error("E023", span=stmt.span,
397                                                  found=type_to_string(val_type),
398                                                  expected=type_to_string(self.current_function_return_type)))
399        elif self.current_function_return_type and not isinstance(self.current_function_return_type, VoidType):
400            # returning nothing from non-void function
401            self.errors.append(make_error("E023", span=stmt.span,
402                                          found="Void",
403                                          expected=type_to_string(self.current_function_return_type)))
404
405    def _check_assign_stmt(self, stmt: AssignStmt):
406        if isinstance(stmt.target, Identifier):
407            name = stmt.target.name
408            info = self.scope.lookup(name)
409            if info is None:
410                # Check "did you mean?"
411                all_names = list(self.scope.bindings.keys()) if self.scope else []
412                s: Scope | None = self.scope
413                while s:
414                    all_names.extend(s.bindings.keys())
415                    s = s.parent
416                suggestions = _nearest_match(name, all_names)
417                msg = f"Unknown identifier: '{name}'"
418                if suggestions:
419                    msg += f" Did you mean: {', '.join(suggestions)}?"
420                self.errors.append(Diagnostic("E011", msg, stmt.span, "error"))
421                return
422            if not info.get("is_mut", False):
423                self.errors.append(make_error("E020", span=stmt.span, name=name))
424        self._check_expr(stmt.value)
425
426    def _check_symbol_stmt(self, stmt: SymbolStmt):
427        if not self.scope.declare(
428                stmt.symbol_name,
429                {"type": StringType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "symbol"}):
430            self.errors.append(
431                make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.symbol_name))
432
433    def _check_import_stmt(self, stmt: ImportStmt):
434        alias = stmt.alias or stmt.module_path
435        self.scope.declare(alias, {"type": AnyType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "module"})
436
437    def _check_enum_decl(self, stmt: EnumDecl):
438        if not self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {"type": EnumType(stmt.name, stmt.variants), "is_mut": False, "kind": "enum"}):
439            self.errors.append(make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.name))
440
441    def _check_struct_decl(self, stmt: StructDecl):
442        field_types = {}
443        for fname, ftype in stmt.fields:
444            field_types[fname] = type_from_name(ftype) if ftype else AnyType()
445        if not self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {"type": StructType(stmt.name, field_types), "is_mut": False, "kind": "struct"}):
446            self.errors.append(make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.name))
447
448    def _check_interface_decl(self, stmt: InterfaceDecl):
449        method_sigs = {}
