450        for mname, params, _ret_type in stmt.methods:
451            method_sigs[mname] = [type_from_name(p[1]) if p[1] else AnyType() for p in params]
452        if not self.scope.declare(stmt.name, {"type": InterfaceType(stmt.name, method_sigs), "is_mut": False, "kind": "interface"}):
453            self.errors.append(make_error("E010", span=stmt.span, name=stmt.name))
454
455    def _check_governed_function_decl(self, stmt: GovernedFunctionDecl):
456        # The parser only builds a GovernedFunctionDecl when at least one
457        # contract clause (requires / ensures / invariant) is present, so the
458        # "missing contract" case is structurally impossible here — no runtime
459        # check needed. Type-check the function body like any other.
460        self._check_function_decl(FunctionDecl(
461            name=stmt.name, params=stmt.params,
462            return_type=stmt.return_type, body=stmt.body, span=stmt.span
463        ))
464
465    def _check_expr(self, expr: Expr) -> Type:
466        """Type-check an expression and return its Type."""
467        if isinstance(expr, IntLiteral):
468            return IntType()
469        elif isinstance(expr, FloatLiteral):
470            return FloatType()
471        elif isinstance(expr, StringLiteral):
472            return StringType()
473        elif isinstance(expr, BoolLiteral):
474            return BoolType()
475        elif isinstance(expr, NoneLiteral):
476            return VoidType()
477        elif isinstance(expr, ErrorLiteral):
478            return ErrorType()
479        elif isinstance(expr, QuenchedLiteral):
480            inner = type_from_name(expr.type_param) if expr.type_param else AnyType()
481            return QuenchedType(inner)
482        elif isinstance(expr, AssignStmt):
483            self.errors.append(Diagnostic(
484                "E901", "Assignment cannot be used as an expression",
485                expr.span, "error"))
486            return ErrorType()
487        elif isinstance(expr, Identifier):
488            if expr.name == "__error__":
489                return ErrorType()
490            # `this` is bound at runtime inside fountain methods; its members are
491            # resolved dynamically, so treat the receiver as Any rather than
492            # flagging it as an unknown identifier.
493            if expr.name == "this":
494                return AnyType()
495            info = self.scope.lookup(expr.name)
496            if info is None:
497                suggestions: list[str] = []
498                s: Scope | None = self.scope
499                while s:
500                    suggestions.extend(s.bindings.keys())
501                    s = s.parent
502                matches = _nearest_match(expr.name, list(set(suggestions)))
503                msg = f"Unknown identifier: '{expr.name}'"
504                if matches:
505                    msg += f" Did you mean: {', '.join(matches)}?"
506                self.errors.append(Diagnostic("E011", msg, expr.span, "error"))
507                return ErrorType()
508            return cast(Type, info.get("type", AnyType()))
509        elif isinstance(expr, BinaryOp):
510            left_type = self._check_expr(expr.left)
511            right_type = self._check_expr(expr.right)
512            # Comparison ops return Bool
513            if expr.op in (TokenType.EQEQ, TokenType.NE, TokenType.LT,
514                           TokenType.GT, TokenType.LE, TokenType.GE):
515                if not isinstance(left_type, type(right_type)) and \
516                   not isinstance(left_type, AnyType) and not isinstance(right_type, AnyType):
517                    self.errors.append(make_error("E022", span=expr.span,
518                                                  op=expr.op.name,
519                                                  expected=type_to_string(left_type),
520                                                  found=type_to_string(right_type)))
521                return BoolType()
522            # Logical ops return Bool
523            if expr.op in (TokenType.AND, TokenType.OR):
524                return BoolType()
525            # Arithmetic ops
526            if not is_assignable(right_type, left_type) and \
527               not isinstance(left_type, AnyType) and not isinstance(right_type, AnyType):
528                self.errors.append(make_error("E022", span=expr.span,
529                                              op=expr.op.name,
530                                              expected=type_to_string(left_type),
531                                              found=type_to_string(right_type)))
532            if isinstance(left_type, FloatType) or isinstance(right_type, FloatType):
533                return FloatType()
534            return left_type
535        elif isinstance(expr, LambdaExpr):
536            self.enter_scope()
537            for pname, ptype in expr.params:
538                t = type_from_name(ptype) if ptype else AnyType()
539                self.scope.declare(
540                    pname, {"type": t, "is_mut": False, "kind": "param"})
541            self._check_block(expr.body)
542            self.exit_scope()
543            return AnyType()
544        elif isinstance(expr, UnaryOp):
545            operand_type = self._check_expr(expr.operand)
546            if expr.op == TokenType.NOT:
547                return BoolType()
548            return operand_type
549        elif isinstance(expr, CallExpr):
550            # E053: a governed function may not be called from core mode.
551            if (isinstance(expr.callee, Identifier)
552                    and expr.callee.name in self._governed_names
553                    and not self.in_governed_mode):
554                self.errors.append(make_error(
555                    "E053", span=expr.span, name=expr.callee.name))
556            callee_type = self._check_expr(expr.callee)
557            if isinstance(callee_type, FunctionType):
558                expected = len(callee_type.param_types)
559                got = len(expr.args)
560                if expected != got and not (expected == 0 and isinstance(callee_type, AnyType)):
561                    self.errors.append(make_error("E030", span=expr.span,
562                                                  name=str(expr.callee),
563                                                  expected=expected, got=got))
564                # Check argument types
565                for i, arg in enumerate(expr.args):
566                    if i < len(callee_type.param_types):
567                        arg_type = self._check_expr(arg)
568                        if not is_assignable(arg_type, callee_type.param_types[i]):
569                            self.errors.append(make_error("E021", span=arg.span if hasattr(arg, 'span') else expr.span,
570                                                          found=type_to_string(arg_type),
571                                                          expected=type_to_string(callee_type.param_types[i])))
572                return callee_type.return_type
573            # Check args anyway
574            for arg in expr.args:
575                self._check_expr(arg)
576            return AnyType()
577        elif isinstance(expr, PipeExpr):
578            left_type = self._check_expr(expr.left)
579            right_type = self._check_expr(expr.right)
580            return right_type
581        elif isinstance(expr, GuardExpr):
582            cond_type = self._check_expr(expr.condition)
583            if not isinstance(cond_type, BoolType) and not isinstance(cond_type, AnyType):
584                self.errors.append(make_error("E024", span=expr.condition.span,
585                                              found=type_to_string(cond_type)))
586            return self._check_expr(expr.expr)
587        elif isinstance(expr, FloodExpr):
588            return ReservoirType(AnyType())
589        elif isinstance(expr, DripExpr):
590            return AnyType()
591        elif isinstance(expr, EvaporateExpr):
592            return VoidType()
593        elif isinstance(expr, CondenseExpr):
594            return ReservoirType(AnyType())
595        elif isinstance(expr, ArrayLiteral):
596            elem = self._check_expr(expr.elements[0]) if expr.elements else AnyType()
597            return ReservoirType(elem)
598        elif isinstance(expr, MemberAccess):
599            self._check_expr(expr.obj)
600            return AnyType()
601        elif isinstance(expr, SanitizeExpr):
602            return self._check_expr(expr.expr)
603        elif isinstance(expr, ArmorExpr):
604            return self._check_expr(expr.expr)
605        elif isinstance(expr, NewExpr):
606            return AnyType()
607        elif isinstance(expr, CascadeCall):
608            inner = self._check_expr(expr.expr)
609            return TaskType(inner)
610        elif isinstance(expr, SymbolExpr):
611            return StringType()
612        elif isinstance(expr, PipelineExpr):
613            left_type = self._check_expr(expr.left)
614            right_type = self._check_expr(expr.right)
615            return right_type
616        elif isinstance(expr, CombineExpr):
617            self._check_expr(expr.left)
618            self._check_expr(expr.right)
619            return StringType()
620        return AnyType()

Semantic analysis and type checking for Thirsty-Lang AST.

scope
errors: list[Diagnostic]
current_function_return_type: Type | None
in_governed_mode
imported_names: dict[str, str]
def enter_scope(self): 
148    def enter_scope(self):
149        self.scope = Scope(self.scope)
def exit_scope(self): 
151    def exit_scope(self):
152        if self.scope.parent:
153            self.scope = self.scope.parent
def check_all( self, ast: Program) -> list[Diagnostic]: 
155    def check_all(self, ast: Program) -> list[Diagnostic]:
156        """Type-check an entire Program AST."""
157        self.errors = []
158        # Register mode
159        if ast.header:
160            self.in_governed_mode = (ast.header.mode == "governed")
161            # Register module name
162            self.scope.declare("__module__", {"type": StringType(), "is_mut": False, "kind": "module"})
163        # Register builtins
164        self._register_builtins()
165        # Hoist top-level function/class names so forward references resolve.
166        self._hoist_declarations(ast.stmts)
167        # Check all statements
168        for stmt in ast.stmts:
169            self._check_stmt(stmt)
170        return self.errors

Type-check an entire Program AST.

def check_ast( ast: Program) -> list[Diagnostic]: 
623def check_ast(ast: Program) -> list[Diagnostic]:
624    """Convenience function to type-check a Program AST."""
625    checker = Checker()
626    return checker.check_all(ast)

Convenience function to type-check a Program AST.

class Interpreter: 
 165class Interpreter:
 166    """Tree-walking interpreter with TCO, governance, and async support."""
 167
 168    def __init__(self, opt_level: int = 0, debug_mode: bool = False):
 169        self.env = Environment()
 170        self.opt_level = opt_level
 171        self.debug_mode = debug_mode
 172        self.tco_enabled = opt_level >= 1
 173        self.mode = "core"
 174        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
 175        self._last_span = None
 176        # Governance wiring (Part A): optional policy engine + authority context.
 177        self.tarl_runtime = None
 178        self.authority = None
 179        # The verified credential (if any) behind ``authority``, retained so the
 180        # capability broker can be built with the same authenticity it sees here.
 181        self._verified_authority: Any = None
 182        # Optional filesystem confinement for governed file capabilities; when
 183        # set, ``thirst::fs`` targets are confined to these roots (C042).
 184        self.path_guard: Any = None
 185        # Authority provenance: a bare string authority is self-asserted
 186        # (authenticated=False, no grants); a verified signed claim sets these.
 187        self.authority_authenticated = False
 188        self.authority_grants: tuple[str, ...] = ()
 189        # Hardened posture: when True, governed gates additionally require an
 190        # authenticated authority and Ed25519-signed proofs, or they fail closed.
 191        self.hardened = False
 192        self._last_proof = None
 193        self._register_builtins()
 194        if self.opt_level >= 2:
 195            self._inline_simple_builtins()
 196        if self.opt_level >= 3:
 197            self._precompute_constants()
 198
 199    def _precompute_constants(self):
 200        """Opt level 3: Pre-compute constant expressions at init time."""
 201        self._const_cache = {}
 202
 203    def _inline_simple_builtins(self):
 204        """Opt level 2: Inline simple builtin functions for faster execution."""
 205        self._inlineable_builtins = set()
 206        for name in ("length", "size", "abs", "print", "pour", "push", "pop", "flood"):
 207            if name in self.env.vars:
 208                self._inlineable_builtins.add(name)
 209
 210    def _register_builtins(self):
 211        """Register builtin functions in the global environment."""
 212        builtins_map = {
 213            "length": lambda x: len(x) if hasattr(x, '__len__') else 0,
 214            "contains": lambda x, y: y in x if hasattr(x, '__contains__') else False,
 215            "split": lambda s, sep=None: s.split(sep) if isinstance(s, str) else [],
 216            "abs": lambda x: abs(x) if hasattr(x, '__abs__') else 0,
 217            "min": lambda *args: min(args) if args else 0,
 218            "max": lambda *args: max(args) if args else 0,
 219            "push": lambda r, v: (r.append(v), len(r))[-1] if isinstance(r, list) else 0,  # type: ignore[func-returns-value]
 220            "pop": lambda r: r.pop() if isinstance(r, list) and r else None,
 221            "size": lambda x: len(x) if hasattr(x, '__len__') else 0,
 222            "get": lambda x, i: x[i] if hasattr(x, '__getitem__') else None,
 223            "flood": lambda r, v: (r.append(v), r)[-1] if isinstance(r, list) else r,  # type: ignore[func-returns-value]
 224            "condense": lambda q: q.get("value") if isinstance(q, dict) and "value" in q else None,
 225            "evaporate": lambda q: q.pop("value") if isinstance(q, dict) and "value" in q else None,
 226            "strain": lambda x: x,
 227            "transmute": lambda x, t: x,
 228            "distill": lambda x: x,
 229            "print": self._builtin_print,
 230            "pour": self._builtin_pour,
 231        }
 232        for name, func in builtins_map.items():
 233            self.env.define(name, func, is_mut=False)
 234
 235    def _builtin_print(self, *args):
 236        self._gate_capability("write", "stdout")
 237        print(*args)
 238        return args[-1] if args else None
 239
 240    def _builtin_pour(self, *args):
 241        self._gate_capability("write", "stdout")
 242        print(*args)
 243        return args[-1] if args else None
 244
 245    def interpret(self, ast: Program, mode: str = "core",
 246                  force_mode: bool = False) -> object:
 247        """Interpret an entire Program AST.
 248
 249        ``force_mode`` keeps ``mode`` even when the program declares a different
 250        one in its header — used when importing a ``.thirsty`` module under a
 251        governed caller, so the imported module's own ``: core`` header cannot
 252        downgrade out of the caller's governance.
 253        """
 254        self.mode = mode
 255        if ast.header and not force_mode:
 256            self.mode = ast.header.mode
 257        # Fail-closed: a governed program the parser flagged as malformed must
 258        # not execute. The parser already discarded its statements; refuse the
 259        # whole program with a denial proof rather than running an empty body.
 260        if self.mode == "governed" and getattr(ast, "parse_failed", False):
 261            from utf.tarl.spec import TarlVerdict
 262            proof = self._make_decision_proof(
 263                source="<fail-closed: governed module failed to parse>",
 264                context={"action": "execute", "reason": "parse_failed"},
 265                verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY, matched_condition="",
 266                trace=[{"kind": "fail-closed", "action": "execute",
 267                        "reason": "governed module had parse errors; "
 268                                  "execution refused"}])
 269            self._last_proof = proof
 270            raise GovernanceViolation(
 271                "<module>",
 272                "fail-closed: governed module failed to parse; refusing to "
 273                "execute recovered statements",
 274                proof)
 275        result = None
 276        try:
 277            for stmt in ast.stmts:
 278                result = self._execute(stmt)
 279                if isinstance(result, (ReturnException, SpillageException)):
 280                    break
 281        except ReturnException as e:
 282            result = e.value
 283        except SpillageException as e:
 284            result = e.value
 285        return result
 286
 287    def _with_debug(self, stmt_or_expr, context: str = ""):
 288        """Wrapper that adds source-location tracking in debug mode."""
 289        if not self.debug_mode:
 290            return None  # No wrapping needed
 291        span = getattr(stmt_or_expr, 'span', None)
 292        if span:
 293            self._last_span = span
 294        return f"at line {span[0]}, column {span[1]}" if span else None
 295
 296    def _execute(self, stmt: Stmt) -> object:
 297        """Execute a statement. Returns a value for expressions or None."""
 298        if self.debug_mode:
 299            try:
 300                return self._execute_impl(stmt)
 301            except Exception as e:
 302                loc = self._with_debug(stmt)
 303                loc_msg = f" at {loc}" if loc else ""
 304                raise type(e)(f"{e}{loc_msg}") from e
 305        else:
 306            return self._execute_impl(stmt)
 307
 308    def _evaluate(self, expr: Expr) -> Any:
 309        """Evaluate an expression and return its value."""
 310        if self.debug_mode:
 311            try:
 312                return self._evaluate_impl(expr)
 313            except Exception as e:
 314                loc = self._with_debug(expr)
 315                loc_msg = f" at {loc}" if loc else ""
 316                raise type(e)(f"{e}{loc_msg}") from e
 317        else:
 318            return self._evaluate_impl(expr)
 319
 320    def _execute_impl(self, stmt: Stmt) -> object:
 321        """Execute a statement. Returns a value for expressions or None."""
 322        if isinstance(stmt, BlockStmt):
 323            return self._execute_block(stmt)
 324        elif isinstance(stmt, ExprStmt):
 325            return self._evaluate(stmt.expr)
 326        elif isinstance(stmt, VariableDecl):
 327            return self._execute_variable_decl(stmt)
 328        elif isinstance(stmt, AssignStmt):
 329            return self._execute_assign(stmt)
 330        elif isinstance(stmt, SymbolStmt):
 331            return self._execute_symbol_stmt(stmt)
 332        elif isinstance(stmt, IfStmt):
 333            return self._execute_if(stmt)
 334        elif isinstance(stmt, WhileStmt):
 335            return self._execute_while(stmt)
 336        elif isinstance(stmt, ForStmt):
 337            return self._execute_for(stmt)
 338        elif isinstance(stmt, TimesStmt):
 339            return self._execute_times(stmt)
 340        elif isinstance(stmt, ReturnStmt):
 341            return self._execute_return(stmt)
 342        elif isinstance(stmt, PourStmt):
 343            return self._execute_pour(stmt)
 344        elif isinstance(stmt, SipStmt):
 345            return self._execute_sip(stmt)
 346        elif isinstance(stmt, ImportStmt):
 347            return self._execute_import(stmt)
 348        elif isinstance(stmt, FunctionDecl):
 349            return self._execute_function_decl(stmt)
 350        elif isinstance(stmt, ClassDecl):
 351            return self._execute_class_decl(stmt)
 352        elif isinstance(stmt, SpillageStmt):
 353            return self._execute_spillage(stmt)
 354        elif isinstance(stmt, CleanupStmt):
 355            return self._execute_cleanup(stmt)
 356        elif isinstance(stmt, ThrowStmt):
 357            return self._execute_throw(stmt)
 358        elif isinstance(stmt, SecurityBlock):
 359            return self._execute_security_block(stmt)
 360        elif isinstance(stmt, CascadeCall):
 361            return self._execute_cascade(stmt)
 362        elif isinstance(stmt, MorphDef):
 363            return self._execute_morph(stmt)
 364        elif isinstance(stmt, DefendStrat):
 365            return None
 366        elif isinstance(stmt, EnumDecl):
 367            return None
 368        elif isinstance(stmt, StructDecl):
 369            return None
 370        elif isinstance(stmt, InterfaceDecl):
 371            return None
 372        elif isinstance(stmt, GovernedFunctionDecl):
 373            return self._execute_governed_function_decl(stmt)
 374        elif isinstance(stmt, ShadowThirstMutation):
 375            return self._execute_shadow_thirst(stmt)
 376        return None
 377
 378    def _execute_block(self, block: BlockStmt) -> object:
 379        old_env = self.env
 380        self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
 381        result = None
 382        try:
 383            for stmt in block.statements:
 384                result = self._execute(stmt)
 385                if isinstance(result, (ReturnException, SpillageException)):
 386                    break
 387        finally:
 388            self.env = old_env
 389        return result
 390
 391    def _execute_variable_decl(self, stmt: VariableDecl) -> object:
 392        if self.mode == "strict" and stmt.init_expr is None:
 393            raise RuntimeError(
 394                f"'strict' module requires '{stmt.name}' to be initialized")
 395        value = None
 396        if stmt.init_expr:
 397            value = self._evaluate(stmt.init_expr)
 398        self.env.define(stmt.name, value, is_mut=stmt.is_mut)
 399        return None  # drink is a statement, not an expression — no value propagation
 400
 401    def _execute_assign(self, stmt: AssignStmt) -> object:
 402        value = self._evaluate(stmt.value)
 403        if isinstance(stmt.target, Identifier):
 404            self.env.set(stmt.target.name, value)
 405        elif isinstance(stmt.target, MemberAccess):
 406            obj = self._evaluate(stmt.target.obj)
 407            if isinstance(obj, FountainInstance):
 408                obj.fields[stmt.target.member] = value
 409            elif isinstance(obj, dict):
 410                obj[stmt.target.member] = value
 411            else:
 412                raise TypeError(
 413                    f"Cannot assign member '{stmt.target.member}' on "
 414                    f"{type(obj).__name__}")
 415        return value
 416
 417    def _execute_symbol_stmt(self, stmt: SymbolStmt) -> object:
 418        self.env.define(stmt.symbol_name, stmt.symbol_name, is_mut=False)
 419        return None
 420
 421    def _execute_if(self, stmt: IfStmt) -> object:
 422        condition = self._evaluate(stmt.condition)
 423        if condition:
 424            return self._execute(stmt.then_block)
 425        elif stmt.else_block:
 426            if isinstance(stmt.else_block, IfStmt):
 427                return self._execute_if(stmt.else_block)
 428            return self._execute(stmt.else_block)
 429        return None
 430
 431    def _execute_while(self, stmt: WhileStmt) -> object:
 432        result = None
 433        while self._evaluate(stmt.condition):
 434            result = self._execute(stmt.body)
 435            if isinstance(result, (ReturnException, SpillageException)):
 436                return result
 437        return result
 438
 439    def _execute_for(self, stmt: ForStmt) -> object:
 440        iterable = self._evaluate(stmt.iterable)
 441        result = None
 442        if hasattr(iterable, '__iter__'):
 443            for item in iterable:
 444                old_env = self.env
 445                self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
 446                self.env.define(stmt.variable.name, item, is_mut=False)
 447                try:
 448                    result = self._execute(stmt.body)
 449                    if isinstance(result, (ReturnException, SpillageException)):
 450                        return result
 451                finally:
 452                    self.env = old_env
 453        return result
 454
 455    def _execute_times(self, stmt: TimesStmt) -> object:
 456        count = self._evaluate(stmt.count)
 457        result = None
 458        for _ in range(int(count)):
 459            result = self._execute(stmt.body)
 460            if isinstance(result, (ReturnException, SpillageException)):
 461                return result
 462        return result
 463
 464    def _execute_return(self, stmt: ReturnStmt) -> object:
 465        value = self._evaluate(stmt.value) if stmt.value else None
 466        if self.tco_enabled and stmt.value and isinstance(stmt.value, CallExpr):
 467            # TCO: evaluate the call directly instead of raising ReturnException
 468            return self._evaluate_call(stmt.value)
 469        raise ReturnException(value)
 470
 471    def _execute_pour(self, stmt: PourStmt) -> object:
 472        if self.mode == "pure":
 473            raise RuntimeError(
 474                "I/O ('pour') is not allowed in a 'pure' module")
 475        self._gate_capability("write", "stdout")
 476        value = self._evaluate(stmt.value)
 477        print(value)
 478        return None  # pour handles its own output — no value propagation
 479
 480    def _execute_sip(self, stmt: SipStmt) -> object:
 481        if self.mode == "pure":
 482            raise RuntimeError(
 483                "I/O ('sip') is not allowed in a 'pure' module")
 484        self._gate_capability("read", "stdin")
 485        value = input()
 486        if isinstance(stmt.target, Identifier):
 487            self.env.set(stmt.target.name, value)
 488        return value
 489
 490    def _make_decision_proof(self, *, source: str, context: dict,
 491                             verdict, matched_condition: str, trace: list):
 492        """Build an (unsigned) ``TarlProof`` for a governance decision made
 493        outside the policy engine — a fail-closed capability denial or a
 494        design-by-contract verdict. Same certificate shape as a policy proof
 495        (``rule_index = -1``, no rule), so every governed decision carries a
 496        proof. Unsigned; see utf/tarl/spec.py:TarlProof for the signing model.
 497        """
 498        import hashlib
 499        import json
 500        from datetime import UTC, datetime
 501
 502        from utf.tarl.spec import TarlProof
 503        policy_hash = "sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(
 504            source.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
 505        ctx_bytes = json.dumps(
 506            context, sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",", ":")
 507        ).encode("utf-8")
 508        context_hash = "sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(ctx_bytes).hexdigest()
 509        return TarlProof(
 510            policy_hash=policy_hash,
 511            context_hash=context_hash,
 512            rule_index=-1,
 513            matched_condition=matched_condition,
 514            verdict=verdict,
 515            evaluated_at=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(timespec="seconds"),
 516            trace=trace,
 517            signature="",
 518            key_id="",
 519        )
 520
 521    def _gate_capability(
 522        self, action: str, target: str, *, path: str | None = None
 523    ) -> None:
 524        """Capability gate for sensitive operations (imports, I/O) in governed
 525        mode. Fail-closed: in governed mode a gated capability is denied unless
 526        a TARL policy engine + authority are attached AND return ALLOW. With no
 527        policy engine wired, governed mode cannot authorize the capability, so
 528        it is denied with a proof — governed mode never implies authority.
 529        Deny-by-default (no matching rule → DENY) comes from the engine itself.
 530        Core (ungoverned) mode is unaffected.
 531
 532        The actual policy evaluation is delegated to a
 533        :class:`~utf.tarl.broker.CapabilityBroker` so in-language stdlib effects
 534        and out-of-language adapters share one mediation path (invariant #7,
 535        "no adapter side doors"). ``path`` (when given) is brokered through the
 536        path guard so the policy sees the confined canonical filesystem target.
 537        """
 538        if self.mode != "governed":
 539            return
 540        from utf.tarl.broker import CapabilityDenied
 541        from utf.tarl.spec import TarlVerdict
 542        ctx = {"action": action, "target": str(target),
 543               **self._authority_context()}
 544        # Hardened-mode prerequisites (authenticated authority + Ed25519 proofs)
 545        # fail closed before any policy is consulted.
 546        hardened_violation = self._hardened_precheck(action, target)
 547        if hardened_violation is not None:
 548            raise hardened_violation
 549        # Fail-closed: governed but no policy engine/authority to grant it.
 550        if self.tarl_runtime is None or self.authority is None:
 551            proof = self._make_decision_proof(
 552                source="<fail-closed: governed mode without policy engine>",
 553                context=ctx, verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY, matched_condition="",
 554                trace=[{"kind": "fail-closed", "action": action,
 555                        "target": str(target),
 556                        "reason": "no policy engine wired to authorize "
 557                                  "capability in governed mode"}])
 558            self._last_proof = proof
 559            raise GovernanceViolation(
 560                f"{action} {target}",
 561                "fail-closed: governed mode requires a policy engine to "
 562                "authorize this capability (run with --policy)",
 563                proof)
 564        broker = self.make_broker()
 565        try:
 566            if path is not None and self.path_guard is not None:
 567                decision = broker.require_path(action, path)
 568            else:
 569                decision = broker.require(action, target)
 570        except CapabilityDenied as denied:
 571            proof = denied.decision.proof
 572            self._last_proof = proof
 573            raise GovernanceViolation(
 574                f"{action} {target}",
 575                denied.decision.reason
 576                or f"capability denied ({proof.verdict})",
 577                proof) from None
 578        except GovernanceViolation:
 579            raise
 580        except Exception as exc:
 581            # Resource exhaustion / evaluator failure must DENY, never fail open
 582            # (C037), and must surface as a governance denial that spillage
 583            # cannot swallow — not a raw exception.
 584            proof = self._make_decision_proof(
 585                source="<fail-closed: policy evaluation error>",
 586                context=ctx, verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY, matched_condition="",
 587                trace=[{"kind": "fail-closed", "action": action,
 588                        "target": str(target),
 589                        "reason": f"policy evaluation error: {exc}"}])
 590            self._last_proof = proof
 591            raise GovernanceViolation(
 592                f"{action} {target}",
 593                f"fail-closed: policy evaluation error: {exc}", proof) from exc
 594        self._last_proof = decision.proof
 595
 596    def _wrap_imported_module_capabilities(
 597        self, module_path: str, module: object
 598    ) -> Any:
 599        """Wrap sensitive stdlib module functions with runtime capability gates.
 600
 601        Capability actions come from the single-source-of-truth table
 602        ``SENSITIVE_STDLIB_CAPABILITIES`` in ``module_system`` so every sensitive
 603        callable carries an explicit ``read``/``write``/``network``/``execute``
 604        action; the gate routing here only consumes that metadata.
 605        """
 606        if self.mode != "governed" or not isinstance(module, dict):
 607            return module
 608
 609        policy = SENSITIVE_STDLIB_CAPABILITIES.get(module_path, {})
 610        if not policy:
 611            return module
 612
 613        wrapped = dict(module)
 614        for name, action in policy.items():
 615            fn = wrapped.get(name)
 616            if not callable(fn):
 617                continue
 618
 619            # Filesystem targets carry a real path in arg 0 — broker them through
 620            # the path guard so traversal/symlink escapes fail closed (C042).
 621            is_fs = module_path == "thirst::fs"
 622
 623            def guarded(*args, __fn=fn, __name=name, __action=action,
 624                        __is_fs=is_fs):
 625                target_detail = args[0] if args else ""
 626                target = f"{module_path}.{__name}"
 627                if target_detail != "":
 628                    target = f"{target}:{target_detail}"
 629                path = (target_detail if __is_fs and isinstance(target_detail, str)
 630                        and target_detail != "" else None)
 631                self._gate_capability(__action, target, path=path)
 632                return __fn(*args)
 633
 634            wrapped[name] = guarded
 635        return wrapped
 636
 637    def _import_thirsty_file(self, path: str) -> dict:
 638        """Import a ``.thirsty`` source file under the caller's governance.
 639
 640        The detached, core-mode interpreter that ``resolve_import`` uses for
 641        file imports is a governance bypass: an imported module's top-level
 642        effects run ungoverned, and the function closures it returns execute
 643        ungoverned when later called from governed code. Instead, run the
 644        imported source on a *child* interpreter that inherits this caller's
 645        policy engine and authority and is forced into governed mode, so
 646
 647          * top-level gated effects (e.g. ``pour``) hit the capability gate
 648            during import, and
 649          * the returned function closures gate when invoked from governed code.
 650
 651        Returns the imported module's new top-level bindings as a dict (the same
 652        shape stdlib modules use). The governed path deliberately bypasses the
 653        global ``ModuleCache`` to avoid leaking governed-bound closures into a
 654        later core-mode import of the same path.
 655        """
 656        import os
 657
 658        from utf.thirsty_lang.lexer import Lexer
 659        from utf.thirsty_lang.parser import Parser
 660        if not os.path.exists(path):
 661            raise ImportError(f"File not found: {path}")
 662        with open(path) as f:
 663            source = f.read()
 664        parser = Parser(Lexer(source).lex())
 665        ast = parser.parse()
 666        if parser.errors:
 667            raise ImportError(f"Failed to import '{path}': {parser.errors[0]}")
 668
 669        child = Interpreter()
 670        child.attach_tarl(self.tarl_runtime)
 671        child.set_authority(self.authority)
 672        baseline = set(child.env.vars)  # builtins to exclude from the module
 673        # Force governed mode regardless of the imported module's own header so
 674        # its effects are gated by *this* caller's policy, not its declaration.
 675        child.interpret(ast, mode="governed", force_mode=True)
 676        return {
 677            name: value
 678            for name, value in child.env.vars.items()
 679            if name not in baseline
 680        }
 681
 682    def _execute_import(self, stmt: ImportStmt) -> object:
 683        self._gate_capability("import", stmt.module_path)
 684        try:
 685            module: object
 686            if (self.mode == "governed"
 687                    and stmt.module_path.endswith(".thirsty")):
 688                module = self._import_thirsty_file(stmt.module_path)
 689            else:
 690                module = resolve_import(stmt.module_path)
 691            module = self._wrap_imported_module_capabilities(
 692                stmt.module_path, module
 693            )
 694            alias = stmt.alias or stmt.module_path
 695            self.env.define(alias, module, is_mut=False)
 696            return module
 697        except GovernanceViolation:
 698            # A denied effect during imported-module execution is a governance
 699            # decision, not an import error — it must propagate, never be
 700            # swallowed into a SpillageException that handlers could catch.
 701            raise
 702        except Exception as e:
 703            raise SpillageException(str(e)) from e
 704
 705    def _make_closure(self, params, body):
 706        # Capture the defining environment so the function closes over its
 707        # lexical scope (enclosing locals), rather than over whatever scope
 708        # happens to be active at call time.
 709        def_env = self.env
 710
 711        def fn(*args):
 712            old_env = self.env
 713            self.env = def_env.enter_scope()
 714            for i, (pname, _) in enumerate(params):
 715                val = args[i] if i < len(args) else None
 716                self.env.define(pname, val, is_mut=False)
 717            result = None
 718            try:
 719                result = self._execute(body)
 720            except ReturnException as e:
 721                result = e.value
 722            finally:
 723                self.env = old_env
 724            return result
 725        return fn
 726
 727    def _execute_function_decl(self, stmt: FunctionDecl) -> object:
 728        fn = self._make_closure(stmt.params, stmt.body)
 729        self.env.define(stmt.name, fn, is_mut=False)
 730        return fn
 731
 732    def _execute_class_decl(self, stmt: ClassDecl) -> object:
 733        def constructor(*args):
 734            instance = FountainInstance(stmt, self.env)
 735            old_env = self.env
 736            self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
 737            self.env.define("this", instance, is_mut=False)
 738            # Apply field default initializers before running init.
 739            for field_spec in stmt.fields:
 740                default_expr = field_spec[2] if len(field_spec) > 2 else None
 741                if default_expr is not None:
 742                    instance.fields[field_spec[0]] = \
 743                        self._evaluate(default_expr)
 744            for method in stmt.methods:
 745                if method.name == "init":
 746                    self._call_user_fn(method, list(args), this_obj=instance)
 747            self.env = old_env
 748            return instance
 749        self.env.define(stmt.name, constructor, is_mut=False)
 750        return constructor
 751
 752    def _execute_spillage(self, stmt: SpillageStmt) -> object:
 753        try:
 754            return self._execute(stmt.body)
 755        except (ReturnException, GovernanceViolation):
 756            # Control flow (return) and governance denials are not errors —
 757            # they must propagate, never be caught by spillage handlers.
 758            raise
 759        except SpillageException as exc:
 760            for error_var, handler in stmt.handlers:
 761                return self._run_spillage_handler(error_var, exc.value, handler)
 762            raise
 763        except Exception as exc:
 764            for error_var, handler in stmt.handlers:
 765                return self._run_spillage_handler(error_var, exc, handler)
 766            raise
 767
 768    def _run_spillage_handler(self, error_var, err_value, handler) -> object:
 769        """Run a spillage error handler, binding the thrown value to the
 770        handler's optional name (``error (name) { ... }``)."""
 771        if error_var is None:
 772            return self._execute(handler)
 773        old_env = self.env
 774        self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
 775        self.env.define(error_var, err_value, is_mut=False)
 776        try:
 777            return self._execute(handler)
 778        finally:
 779            self.env = old_env
 780
 781    def _execute_cleanup(self, stmt: CleanupStmt) -> object:
 782        try:
 783            return self._execute(stmt.body)
 784        finally:
 785            self._execute(stmt.finalizer)
 786
 787    def _execute_throw(self, stmt: ThrowStmt) -> object:
 788        value = self._evaluate(stmt.value)
 789        raise SpillageException(value)
 790
 791    def _execute_security_block(self, stmt: SecurityBlock) -> object:
 792        return self._execute(stmt.body)
 793
 794    def _execute_cascade(self, stmt: CascadeCall) -> object:
 795        """cascade <call> — async call with await: runs on the pool and
 796        resolves to the call's value (not a Future)."""
 797        if isinstance(stmt.expr, CallExpr):
 798            callee = self._evaluate(stmt.expr.callee)
 799            args = [self._evaluate(a) for a in stmt.expr.args]
 800            if callable(callee):
 801                future = self.executor.submit(callee, *args)
 802                return future.result()
 803        return self._evaluate(stmt.expr)
 804
 805    def _execute_morph(self, stmt: MorphDef) -> object:
 806        def transform(*args):
 807            old_env = self.env
 808            self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
 809            for i, (pname, _) in enumerate(stmt.params):
 810                val = args[i] if i < len(args) else None
 811                self.env.define(pname, val, is_mut=False)
 812            result = None
 813            try:
 814                result = self._execute(stmt.body)
 815            except ReturnException as e:
 816                result = e.value
 817            finally:
 818                self.env = old_env
 819            return result
 820        self.env.define(stmt.name, transform, is_mut=False)
 821        return transform
 822
 823    def _execute_shadow_thirst(self, stmt: ShadowThirstMutation) -> object:
 824        if stmt.canonical_block:
 825            return self._execute(stmt.canonical_block)
 826        return None
 827
 828    def attach_tarl(self, runtime) -> "Interpreter":
 829        """Wire a TARL policy engine for governed-function routing.
 830
 831        ``runtime`` is a ``utf.tarl.runtime.TarlRuntime``.  Passing it here
 832        (rather than importing at module load) keeps thirsty_lang decoupled
 833        from tarl unless governance is actually used.  Returns self.
 834        """
 835        self.tarl_runtime = runtime
 836        return self
 837
 838    def set_authority(self, authority) -> "Interpreter":
 839        """Set a *self-asserted* authority (a bare string subject).
 840
 841        This carries no authenticity: ``authority_authenticated`` stays False and
 842        no grants are bound. In hardened mode such an authority cannot pass a
 843        gated capability. Use :meth:`set_verified_authority` for a signed
 844        credential. Passing a ``VerifiedAuthority`` here is also accepted.
 845        """
 846        from utf.tarl.authority import VerifiedAuthority
 847        if isinstance(authority, VerifiedAuthority):
 848            return self.set_verified_authority(authority)
 849        self.authority = authority
 850        self.authority_authenticated = False
 851        self.authority_grants = ()
 852        self._verified_authority = None
 853        return self
 854
 855    def set_verified_authority(self, verified) -> "Interpreter":
 856        """Bind an authenticated authority resolved from a signed claim.
 857
 858        ``verified`` is a ``utf.tarl.authority.VerifiedAuthority``. Its subject,
 859        grants, and authenticity flag are injected into every governance context.
 860        """
 861        self.authority = verified.subject
 862        self.authority_authenticated = bool(verified.authenticated)
 863        self.authority_grants = tuple(verified.grants)
 864        self._verified_authority = verified
 865        return self
 866
 867    def set_hardened(self, hardened: bool = True) -> "Interpreter":
 868        """Enable the hardened posture (authenticated authority + Ed25519 proofs
 869        required at every governed gate, else fail closed)."""
 870        self.hardened = hardened
 871        return self
 872
 873    def set_path_guard(self, roots) -> "Interpreter":
 874        """Confine governed ``thirst::fs`` targets to ``roots`` (C042).
 875
 876        ``roots`` is an iterable of allowed directory roots, or a ready
 877        ``utf.tarl.pathguard.PathGuard``. Once set, file capabilities are
 878        brokered on the *canonical* path, so traversal/symlink escapes fail
 879        closed before the policy is consulted. With no guard set, file targets
 880        are brokered as-is (unchanged behaviour)."""
 881        from utf.tarl.pathguard import PathGuard
 882        if roots is None or isinstance(roots, PathGuard):
 883            self.path_guard = roots
 884        else:
 885            self.path_guard = PathGuard(list(roots))
 886        return self
 887
 888    def make_broker(self):
 889        """Build a :class:`~utf.tarl.broker.CapabilityBroker` bound to this
 890        interpreter's runtime, authority, hardened posture, and path guard.
 891
 892        This is the *single* mediation point: the in-language capability gate
 893        (:meth:`_gate_capability`) and any out-of-language adapters (FFI, MCP/
 894        agent tools, subprocess wrappers) all broker through an identically
 895        configured broker, so there is one enforcement path, not two."""
 896        from utf.tarl.broker import CapabilityBroker
 897        authority = (self._verified_authority
 898                     if self._verified_authority is not None
 899                     else (self.authority or ""))
 900        return CapabilityBroker(
 901            self.tarl_runtime, authority=authority,
 902            require_authenticated=self.hardened, path_guard=self.path_guard)
 903
 904    def _authority_context(self) -> dict:
 905        """Authority fields merged into every governance evaluation context."""
 906        return {
 907            "authority": self.authority or "",
 908            "authority_subject": self.authority or "",
 909            "authority_authenticated": self.authority_authenticated,
 910            "authority_grants": list(self.authority_grants),
 911        }
 912
 913    def _hardened_precheck(self, action: str, target: str):
 914        """Hardened-mode gate prerequisites, evaluated before policy routing.
 915
 916        Returns a fail-closed ``GovernanceViolation`` to raise, or None when the
 917        prerequisites hold. Enforces: an authenticated authority, and a runtime
 918        configured to emit Ed25519-signed proofs (non-repudiable audit).
 919        """
 920        if not self.hardened:
 921            return None
 922        from utf.tarl.spec import TarlVerdict
 923        reason = None
 924        if not self.authority_authenticated:
 925            reason = ("hardened mode requires an authenticated (signed) "
 926                      "authority; a self-asserted authority is not sufficient")
 927        elif getattr(self.tarl_runtime, "_signing_alg", "") != "ed25519":
 928            reason = ("hardened mode requires Ed25519-signed proofs; configure "
 929                      "an Ed25519 signing key on the runtime")
 930        if reason is None:
 931            return None
 932        proof = self._make_decision_proof(
 933            source="<fail-closed: hardened-mode prerequisite>",
 934            context={**self._authority_context(), "action": action,
 935                     "target": str(target)},
 936            verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY, matched_condition="",
 937            trace=[{"kind": "fail-closed", "action": action,
 938                    "target": str(target), "reason": reason}])
 939        self._last_proof = proof
 940        return GovernanceViolation(f"{action} {target}", reason, proof)
 941
 942    def _execute_governed_function_decl(self, stmt: GovernedFunctionDecl):
 943        """Define a top-level governed function with full contract enforcement."""
 944        def fn(*args):
 945            old_env = self.env
 946            self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
 947            context = {}
 948            for i, (pname, _) in enumerate(stmt.params):
 949                val = args[i] if i < len(args) else None
 950                self.env.define(pname, val, is_mut=False)
 951                context[pname] = val
 952            try:
 953                return self._run_governed(stmt, context, top_level=True)
 954            finally:
 955                self.env = old_env
 956        self.env.define(stmt.name, fn, is_mut=False)
 957        return fn
 958
 959    def _run_governed(self, decl: GovernedFunctionDecl, context: dict,
 960                      top_level: bool):
 961        """Enforce entry contracts, run the body, then enforce exit contracts.
 962
 963        Params are assumed already bound in the current scope. ``top_level``
 964        gates the cross-mode guard (E053): it applies to module-level governed
 965        functions, not to method contracts (which are design-by-contract
 966        assertions valid in any mode).
 967        """
 968        allowed, reason, proof = self._enforce_governance(
 969            context, decl, phase="entry", enforce_mode_guard=top_level)
 970        if proof is not None:
 971            self._last_proof = proof
 972        if not allowed:
 973            raise GovernanceViolation(decl.name, reason, proof)
 974
 975        result = None
 976        try:
 977            result = self._execute(decl.body)
 978        except ReturnException as e:
 979            result = e.value
 980
 981        # Exit contracts: bind `result` so ensures/invariant can reference it.
 982        self.env.define("result", result, is_mut=False)
 983        exit_ctx = dict(context)
 984        exit_ctx["result"] = result
 985        allowed, reason, proof = self._enforce_governance(
 986            exit_ctx, decl, phase="exit", enforce_mode_guard=top_level)
 987        if proof is not None:
 988            self._last_proof = proof
 989        if not allowed:
 990            raise GovernanceViolation(decl.name, reason, proof)
 991        return result
 992
 993    def _eval_predicate(self, expr, annotation, label):
 994        """Evaluate a governance predicate; return (ok, reason)."""
 995        try:
 996            if self._evaluate(expr):
 997                return (True, "")
 998            return (False, f"{label} failed: {annotation or '<expr>'}")
 999        except Exception as exc:
1000            return (False, f"{label} error: {exc}")
1001
1002    def _contract_predicates(self, decl, phase):
1003        """Yield (expr, annotation, label) for the contract predicates that
1004        apply at this boundary: requires + invariant on entry, ensures +
1005        invariant on exit. Empty when ``decl`` carries no contract.
1006        """
1007        if decl is None:
1008            return
1009        if phase == "entry":
1010            if getattr(decl, "requires_expr", None) is not None:
1011                yield (decl.requires_expr, decl.requires_annotation,
1012                       "precondition")
1013            if getattr(decl, "invariant_expr", None) is not None:
1014                yield (decl.invariant_expr, decl.invariant_annotation,
1015                       "invariant (entry)")
1016        else:
1017            if getattr(decl, "ensures_expr", None) is not None:
1018                yield (decl.ensures_expr, decl.ensures_annotation,
1019                       "postcondition")
1020            if getattr(decl, "invariant_expr", None) is not None:
1021                yield (decl.invariant_expr, decl.invariant_annotation,
1022                       "invariant (exit)")
1023
1024    def _make_contract_proof(self, decl, phase, context, verdict,
1025                             matched_condition, trace):
1026        """Proof certificate for a design-by-contract verdict (Fix: every
1027        governed boundary decision carries a proof, not just policy ones)."""
1028        name = getattr(decl, "name", "<anon>") if decl is not None else "<anon>"
1029        source = f"contract:{name}:{phase}"
1030        return self._make_decision_proof(
1031            source=source, context=context, verdict=verdict,
1032            matched_condition=matched_condition, trace=trace)
1033
1034    def _enforce_governance(self, context: dict, decl=None, phase="entry",
1035                            enforce_mode_guard=True):
1036        """Decide whether a governed call boundary is allowed.
1037
1038        Returns ``(allowed, reason, proof)``. Design-by-contract predicates
1039        (requires/invariant at entry; ensures/invariant at exit) hold unless a
1040        declared predicate fails. The TARL layer adds deny-by-default access
1041        control when a policy engine + authority are configured. Every decision
1042        on a contract-bearing boundary carries a proof — ALLOW and DENY alike.
1043        """
1044        from utf.tarl.spec import TarlVerdict
1045        proof = None
1046        if phase == "entry":
1047            # Cross-mode guard (E053): top-level governed fn from core is denied.
1048            if decl is not None and enforce_mode_guard and self.mode != "governed":
1049                return (False,
1050                        "governed function invoked outside governed mode", None)
1051
1052        # Design-by-contract predicates for this phase.
1053        trace = []
1054        for expr, ann, label in self._contract_predicates(decl, phase):
1055            ok, reason = self._eval_predicate(expr, ann, label)
1056            trace.append({"predicate": label, "annotation": ann or "<expr>",
1057                          "phase": phase, "result": "pass" if ok else "fail"})
1058            if not ok:
1059                deny = self._make_contract_proof(
1060                    decl, phase, context, TarlVerdict.DENY, ann or "", trace)
1061                self._last_proof = deny
1062                return (False, reason, deny)
1063        if trace:  # contract was checked and held → certify it
1064            proof = self._make_contract_proof(
1065                decl, phase, context, TarlVerdict.ALLOW, "", trace)
1066
1067        if phase == "entry":
1068            # TARL policy routing (deny-by-default access control). A policy
1069            # proof supersedes the contract proof as the boundary certificate.
1070            if self.mode == "governed" and decl is not None and (
1071                    self.tarl_runtime is None or self.authority is None):
1072                reason = (
1073                    "governed function requires a policy engine and authority"
1074                )
1075                deny = self._make_contract_proof(
1076                    decl, phase, context, TarlVerdict.DENY, reason,
1077                    trace + [{"kind": "fail-closed", "phase": phase,
1078                              "reason": reason}])
1079                self._last_proof = deny
1080                return (False, reason, deny)
1081            if self.tarl_runtime is not None and self.authority is not None:
1082                # Hardened-mode prerequisites fail closed before policy routing.
1083                action_name = decl.name if decl is not None else "<call>"
1084                hardened_violation = self._hardened_precheck(
1085                    action_name, action_name)
1086                if hardened_violation is not None:
1087                    return (False, hardened_violation.reason,
1088                            hardened_violation.proof)
1089                policy_ctx = dict(context)
1090                policy_ctx.update(self._authority_context())
1091                if decl is not None:
1092                    policy_ctx.setdefault("action", decl.name)
1093                decision, proof = self.tarl_runtime.evaluate_with_proof(
1094                    policy_ctx)
1095                if decision.verdict != TarlVerdict.ALLOW:
1096                    return (False,
1097                            decision.reason
1098                            or f"policy verdict: {decision.verdict}",
1099                            proof)
1100        return (True, "allowed", proof)
1101
1102    def _call_user_fn(self, func_decl: FunctionDecl, args: list,
1103                      this_obj: object = None) -> object:
1104        old_env = self.env
1105        self.env = self.env.enter_scope()
1106        # Bind the receiver for fountain methods. Two conventions are supported:
1107        #   * the `this` keyword (no receiver parameter declared), and
1108        #   * an explicit leading `self`/`this` parameter.
1109        # `this` is always bound so `this.field` resolves; if the method also
1110        # declares a leading self parameter, it receives the instance and is
1111        # not consumed from the caller's positional arguments.
1112        context = {}
1113        params = list(func_decl.params)
1114        if this_obj is not None:
1115            self.env.define("this", this_obj, is_mut=False)
1116            if params and params[0][0] in ("self", "this"):
1117                recv_name = params[0][0]
1118                self.env.define(recv_name, this_obj, is_mut=False)
1119                context[recv_name] = this_obj
1120                params = params[1:]
1121        for i, (pname, _) in enumerate(params):
1122            val = args[i] if i < len(args) else None
1123            self.env.define(pname, val, is_mut=False)
1124            context[pname] = val
1125        try:
1126            if isinstance(func_decl, GovernedFunctionDecl):
1127                # Method contracts: DBC enforcement, no cross-mode guard.
1128                return self._run_governed(func_decl, context, top_level=False)
1129            result = None
1130            try:
1131                result = self._execute(func_decl.body)
1132            except ReturnException as e:
1133                result = e.value
1134            return result
1135        finally:
1136            self.env = old_env
1137
1138    def _evaluate_impl(self, expr: Expr) -> Any:
1139        """Evaluate an expression and return its value."""
1140        if isinstance(expr, IntLiteral):
1141            return expr.value
1142        elif isinstance(expr, FloatLiteral):
1143            return expr.value
1144        elif isinstance(expr, StringLiteral):
1145            return expr.value
1146        elif isinstance(expr, BoolLiteral):
1147            return expr.value
1148        elif isinstance(expr, NoneLiteral):
1149            return None
1150        elif isinstance(expr, ErrorLiteral):
1151            return Exception(expr.value)
1152        elif isinstance(expr, QuenchedLiteral):
1153            return {"type": expr.type_param, "value": self._evaluate(expr.value) if expr.value else None}
1154        elif isinstance(expr, AssignStmt):
1155            raise RuntimeError("assignment cannot be used as an expression")
1156        elif isinstance(expr, Identifier):
1157            if expr.name == "__error__":
1158                return None
1159            return self.env.get(expr.name)
1160        elif isinstance(expr, BinaryOp):
1161            return self._evaluate_binary(expr)
1162        elif isinstance(expr, UnaryOp):
1163            return self._evaluate_unary(expr)
1164        elif isinstance(expr, LambdaExpr):
1165            return self._make_closure(expr.params, expr.body)
1166        elif isinstance(expr, CallExpr):
1167            return self._evaluate_call(expr)
1168        elif isinstance(expr, PipeExpr):
1169            return self._evaluate_pipe(expr)
1170        elif isinstance(expr, GuardExpr):
1171            return self._evaluate_guard(expr)
1172        elif isinstance(expr, FloodExpr):
1173            return self._evaluate_flood(expr)
1174        elif isinstance(expr, DripExpr):
1175            return self._evaluate_drip(expr)
1176        elif isinstance(expr, EvaporateExpr):
1177            return self._evaluate_evaporate(expr)
1178        elif isinstance(expr, CondenseExpr):
1179            return self._evaluate_condense(expr)
1180        elif isinstance(expr, ArrayLiteral):
1181            return [self._evaluate_impl(e) for e in expr.elements]
1182        elif isinstance(expr, MemberAccess):
1183            return self._evaluate_member(expr)
1184        elif isinstance(expr, SanitizeExpr):
1185            return self._evaluate(expr.expr)
1186        elif isinstance(expr, ArmorExpr):
1187            return self._evaluate(expr.expr)
1188        elif isinstance(expr, NewExpr):
1189            return self._evaluate_new(expr)
1190        elif isinstance(expr, CascadeCall):
1191            return self._execute_cascade(expr)
1192        elif isinstance(expr, SymbolExpr):
1193            return expr.symbol_name
1194        elif isinstance(expr, PipelineExpr):
1195            return self._evaluate_pipeline(expr)
1196        elif isinstance(expr, CombineExpr):
1197            return self._evaluate_combine(expr)
1198        return None
1199
1200    def _evaluate_binary(self, expr: BinaryOp) -> object:
1201        left = self._evaluate_impl(expr.left)
1202        right = self._evaluate_impl(expr.right)
1203        op = expr.op
1204        if op == TokenType.EQEQ:
1205            return left == right
1206        elif op == TokenType.NE:
1207            return left != right
1208        elif op == TokenType.LT:
1209            return left < right
1210        elif op == TokenType.LE:
1211            return left <= right
1212        elif op == TokenType.GT:
1213            return left > right
1214        elif op == TokenType.GE:
1215            return left >= right
1216        elif op == TokenType.PLUS:
1217            return left + right
1218        elif op == TokenType.MINUS:
1219            return left - right
1220        elif op == TokenType.STAR:
1221            return left * right
1222        elif op == TokenType.SLASH:
1223            return left / right
1224        elif op == TokenType.PERCENT:
1225            return left % right
1226        elif op == TokenType.AND:
1227            return left and right
1228        elif op == TokenType.OR:
1229            return left or right
1230        return None
1231
1232    def _evaluate_unary(self, expr: UnaryOp) -> object:
1233        operand = self._evaluate_impl(expr.operand)
1234        if expr.op == TokenType.MINUS:
1235            return -operand
1236        elif expr.op == TokenType.NOT:
1237            return not operand
1238        return operand
1239
1240    def _evaluate_call(self, expr: CallExpr) -> object:
1241        callee = self._evaluate_impl(expr.callee)
1242        args = [self._evaluate_impl(a) for a in expr.args]
1243        if callable(callee):
1244            return callee(*args)
1245        raise TypeError(f"Cannot call non-callable: {callee}")
1246
1247    def _evaluate_pipe(self, expr: PipeExpr) -> object:
1248        left = self._evaluate_impl(expr.left)
1249        right = self._evaluate_impl(expr.right)
1250        if callable(right):
1251            return right(left)
1252        return left
1253
1254    def _evaluate_guard(self, expr: GuardExpr) -> object:
1255        condition = self._evaluate_impl(expr.condition)
1256        if condition:
1257            return self._evaluate_impl(expr.expr)
1258        return None
1259
1260    def _evaluate_flood(self, expr: FloodExpr) -> object:
1261        """flood <target> — fill a reservoir from a value.
1262
1263        If the target is already a reservoir (list) it is returned as-is;
1264        otherwise the value is wrapped into a fresh single-element reservoir.
1265        """
1266        target = self._evaluate_impl(expr.target)
1267        if isinstance(target, list):
1268            return target
1269        return [target]
1270
1271    def _evaluate_drip(self, expr: DripExpr) -> object:
1272        target = self._evaluate_impl(expr.target)
1273        if isinstance(target, list) and target:
1274            return target.pop()
1275        return None
1276
1277    def _evaluate_evaporate(self, expr: EvaporateExpr) -> object:
1278        """evaporate <target> — release a resource; empties a reservoir/map."""
1279        target = self._evaluate_impl(expr.target)
1280        if isinstance(target, (list, dict)):
1281            target.clear()
1282        return None
1283
1284    def _evaluate_condense(self, expr: CondenseExpr) -> object:
1285        target = self._evaluate_impl(expr.target)
1286        if isinstance(target, dict):
1287            return target.get("value")
1288        return None
1289
1290    def _evaluate_member(self, expr: MemberAccess) -> object:
1291        """obj.member — read a fountain field, bind a method, or index a map."""
1292        obj = self._evaluate_impl(expr.obj)
1293        name = expr.member
1294        if isinstance(obj, FountainInstance):
1295            if name in obj.fields:
1296                return obj.fields[name]
1297            method = obj.methods.get(name)
1298            if method is not None:
1299                # Bound method: the instance is bound as `this`, and the call
1300                # arguments map to the method's declared params.
1301                return lambda *a: self._call_user_fn(
1302                    method, list(a), this_obj=obj)
1303            raise NameError(
1304                f"'{obj.cls_decl.name}' has no member '{name}'")
1305        if isinstance(obj, dict):
1306            return obj.get(name)
1307        raise TypeError(
1308            f"Cannot access member '{name}' on {type(obj).__name__}")
1309
1310    def _evaluate_new(self, expr: NewExpr) -> object:
1311        constructor = self.env.get(expr.class_name)
1312        args = [self._evaluate_impl(a) for a in expr.args]
1313        if callable(constructor):
1314            return constructor(*args)
1315        raise TypeError(f"Cannot instantiate non-callable: {expr.class_name}")
1316
1317    def _evaluate_pipeline(self, expr: PipelineExpr) -> object:
1318        """TSCG pipeline ``left -> right``: feed the left value into the right
1319        operand when it is callable (same shape as the ``|`` pipe operator).
1320
1321        ``PipelineExpr`` is a binary node (``left``/``right``); an earlier
1322        version walked a non-existent ``.steps`` list and raised AttributeError
1323        on any ``->`` expression.
1324        """
1325        left = self._evaluate_impl(expr.left)
1326        right = self._evaluate_impl(expr.right)
1327        if callable(right):
1328            return right(left)
1329        return right
1330
1331    def _evaluate_combine(self, expr: CombineExpr) -> object:
1332        left = self._evaluate_impl(expr.left)
1333        right = self._evaluate_impl(expr.right)
1334        if isinstance(left, bool) or isinstance(right, bool):
1335            if expr.op == "^":
1336                return bool(left) and bool(right)
1337            if expr.op == "||":
1338                return bool(left) or bool(right)
1339        if isinstance(left, dict) and isinstance(right, dict):
1340            merged = dict(left)
1341            merged.update(right)
1342            return merged
1343        if isinstance(left, list) and isinstance(right, list):
1344            return left + right
1345        return right

Tree-walking interpreter with TCO, governance, and async support.

Interpreter(opt_level: int = 0, debug_mode: bool = False) 
168    def __init__(self, opt_level: int = 0, debug_mode: bool = False):
169        self.env = Environment()
170        self.opt_level = opt_level
171        self.debug_mode = debug_mode
172        self.tco_enabled = opt_level >= 1
173        self.mode = "core"
174        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
175        self._last_span = None
176        # Governance wiring (Part A): optional policy engine + authority context.
177        self.tarl_runtime = None
178        self.authority = None
179        # The verified credential (if any) behind ``authority``, retained so the
180        # capability broker can be built with the same authenticity it sees here.
181        self._verified_authority: Any = None
182        # Optional filesystem confinement for governed file capabilities; when
183        # set, ``thirst::fs`` targets are confined to these roots (C042).
184        self.path_guard: Any = None
185        # Authority provenance: a bare string authority is self-asserted
186        # (authenticated=False, no grants); a verified signed claim sets these.
187        self.authority_authenticated = False
188        self.authority_grants: tuple[str, ...] = ()
189        # Hardened posture: when True, governed gates additionally require an
190        # authenticated authority and Ed25519-signed proofs, or they fail closed.
191        self.hardened = False
192        self._last_proof = None
193        self._register_builtins()
194        if self.opt_level >= 2:
195            self._inline_simple_builtins()
196        if self.opt_level >= 3:
197            self._precompute_constants()
env
opt_level
debug_mode
tco_enabled
mode
executor
tarl_runtime
authority
path_guard: Any
authority_authenticated
authority_grants: tuple[str, ...]
hardened
def interpret( self, ast: Program, mode: str = 'core', force_mode: bool = False) -> object: 
245    def interpret(self, ast: Program, mode: str = "core",
246                  force_mode: bool = False) -> object:
247        """Interpret an entire Program AST.
248
249        ``force_mode`` keeps ``mode`` even when the program declares a different
250        one in its header — used when importing a ``.thirsty`` module under a
251        governed caller, so the imported module's own ``: core`` header cannot
252        downgrade out of the caller's governance.
253        """
254        self.mode = mode
255        if ast.header and not force_mode:
256            self.mode = ast.header.mode
257        # Fail-closed: a governed program the parser flagged as malformed must
258        # not execute. The parser already discarded its statements; refuse the
259        # whole program with a denial proof rather than running an empty body.
260        if self.mode == "governed" and getattr(ast, "parse_failed", False):
261            from utf.tarl.spec import TarlVerdict
262            proof = self._make_decision_proof(
263                source="<fail-closed: governed module failed to parse>",
264                context={"action": "execute", "reason": "parse_failed"},
265                verdict=TarlVerdict.DENY, matched_condition="",
266                trace=[{"kind": "fail-closed", "action": "execute",
267                        "reason": "governed module had parse errors; "
268                                  "execution refused"}])
269            self._last_proof = proof
270            raise GovernanceViolation(
271                "<module>",
272                "fail-closed: governed module failed to parse; refusing to "
273                "execute recovered statements",
274                proof)
275        result = None
276        try:
277            for stmt in ast.stmts:
278                result = self._execute(stmt)
279                if isinstance(result, (ReturnException, SpillageException)):
280                    break
281        except ReturnException as e:
282            result = e.value
283        except SpillageException as e:
284            result = e.value
285        return result

Interpret an entire Program AST.

force_mode keeps mode even when the program declares a different one in its header — used when importing a .thirsty module under a governed caller, so the imported module's own : core header cannot downgrade out of the caller's governance.

def attach_tarl(self, runtime) -> Interpreter: 
828    def attach_tarl(self, runtime) -> "Interpreter":
829        """Wire a TARL policy engine for governed-function routing.
830
831        ``runtime`` is a ``utf.tarl.runtime.TarlRuntime``.  Passing it here
832        (rather than importing at module load) keeps thirsty_lang decoupled
833        from tarl unless governance is actually used.  Returns self.
834        """
835        self.tarl_runtime = runtime
836        return self

Wire a TARL policy engine for governed-function routing.

runtime is a utf.tarl.runtime.TarlRuntime. Passing it here (rather than importing at module load) keeps thirsty_lang decoupled from tarl unless governance is actually used. Returns self.

def set_authority(self, authority) -> Interpreter: 
838    def set_authority(self, authority) -> "Interpreter":
839        """Set a *self-asserted* authority (a bare string subject).
840
841        This carries no authenticity: ``authority_authenticated`` stays False and
842        no grants are bound. In hardened mode such an authority cannot pass a
843        gated capability. Use :meth:`set_verified_authority` for a signed
844        credential. Passing a ``VerifiedAuthority`` here is also accepted.
845        """
846        from utf.tarl.authority import VerifiedAuthority
847        if isinstance(authority, VerifiedAuthority):
848            return self.set_verified_authority(authority)
849        self.authority = authority
850        self.authority_authenticated = False
851        self.authority_grants = ()
852        self._verified_authority = None
853        return self

Set a self-asserted authority (a bare string subject).

This carries no authenticity: authority_authenticated stays False and no grants are bound. In hardened mode such an authority cannot pass a gated capability. Use set_verified_authority() for a signed credential. Passing a VerifiedAuthority here is also accepted.

def set_verified_authority(self, verified) -> Interpreter: 
855    def set_verified_authority(self, verified) -> "Interpreter":
856        """Bind an authenticated authority resolved from a signed claim.
857
858        ``verified`` is a ``utf.tarl.authority.VerifiedAuthority``. Its subject,
859        grants, and authenticity flag are injected into every governance context.
860        """
861        self.authority = verified.subject
862        self.authority_authenticated = bool(verified.authenticated)
863        self.authority_grants = tuple(verified.grants)
864        self._verified_authority = verified
865        return self

Bind an authenticated authority resolved from a signed claim.

verified is a utf.tarl.authority.VerifiedAuthority. Its subject, grants, and authenticity flag are injected into every governance context.

def set_hardened(self, hardened: bool = True) -> Interpreter: 
867    def set_hardened(self, hardened: bool = True) -> "Interpreter":
868        """Enable the hardened posture (authenticated authority + Ed25519 proofs
869        required at every governed gate, else fail closed)."""
870        self.hardened = hardened
871        return self

Enable the hardened posture (authenticated authority + Ed25519 proofs required at every governed gate, else fail closed).

def set_path_guard(self, roots) -> Interpreter: 
873    def set_path_guard(self, roots) -> "Interpreter":
874        """Confine governed ``thirst::fs`` targets to ``roots`` (C042).
875
876        ``roots`` is an iterable of allowed directory roots, or a ready
877        ``utf.tarl.pathguard.PathGuard``. Once set, file capabilities are
878        brokered on the *canonical* path, so traversal/symlink escapes fail
879        closed before the policy is consulted. With no guard set, file targets
880        are brokered as-is (unchanged behaviour)."""
881        from utf.tarl.pathguard import PathGuard
882        if roots is None or isinstance(roots, PathGuard):
883            self.path_guard = roots
884        else:
885            self.path_guard = PathGuard(list(roots))
886        return self

Confine governed thirst::fs targets to roots (C042).

roots is an iterable of allowed directory roots, or a ready utf.tarl.pathguard.PathGuard. Once set, file capabilities are brokered on the canonical path, so traversal/symlink escapes fail closed before the policy is consulted. With no guard set, file targets are brokered as-is (unchanged behaviour).

def make_broker(self): 
888    def make_broker(self):
889        """Build a :class:`~utf.tarl.broker.CapabilityBroker` bound to this
890        interpreter's runtime, authority, hardened posture, and path guard.
891
892        This is the *single* mediation point: the in-language capability gate
893        (:meth:`_gate_capability`) and any out-of-language adapters (FFI, MCP/
894        agent tools, subprocess wrappers) all broker through an identically
895        configured broker, so there is one enforcement path, not two."""
896        from utf.tarl.broker import CapabilityBroker
897        authority = (self._verified_authority
898                     if self._verified_authority is not None
899                     else (self.authority or ""))
900        return CapabilityBroker(
901            self.tarl_runtime, authority=authority,
902            require_authenticated=self.hardened, path_guard=self.path_guard)

Build a ~utf.tarl.broker.CapabilityBroker bound to this interpreter's runtime, authority, hardened posture, and path guard.

This is the single mediation point: the in-language capability gate (_gate_capability()) and any out-of-language adapters (FFI, MCP/ agent tools, subprocess wrappers) all broker through an identically configured broker, so there is one enforcement path, not two.

class Environment: 
101class Environment:
102    """Scoped variable storage."""
103
104    def __init__(self, parent: 'Environment | None' = None):
105        self.parent = parent
106        self.vars: dict[str, object] = {}
107        self.mutable: dict[str, bool] = {}
108
109    def define(self, name: str, value: object, is_mut: bool = False):
110        self.vars[name] = value
111        self.mutable[name] = is_mut
112
113    def get(self, name: str) -> object:
114        if name in self.vars:
115            return self.vars[name]
116        if self.parent:
117            return self.parent.get(name)
118        raise NameError(f"Undefined variable: '{name}'")
119
120    def set(self, name: str, value: object):
121        if name in self.vars:
122            if not self.mutable.get(name, False):
123                raise TypeError(f"Cannot assign to immutable variable: '{name}'")
124            self.vars[name] = value
125            return
126        if self.parent:
127            self.parent.set(name, value)
128            return
129        raise NameError(f"Undefined variable: '{name}'")
130
131    def has(self, name: str) -> bool:
132        return name in self.vars or bool(self.parent and self.parent.has(name))
133
134    def enter_scope(self) -> 'Environment':
135        return Environment(self)
136
137    def __repr__(self):
138        return f"Environment({list(self.vars.keys())})"

Scoped variable storage.

Environment(parent: Environment | None = None) 
104    def __init__(self, parent: 'Environment | None' = None):
105        self.parent = parent
