106        self.vars: dict[str, object] = {}
107        self.mutable: dict[str, bool] = {}
parent
vars: dict[str, object]
mutable: dict[str, bool]
def define(self, name: str, value: object, is_mut: bool = False): 
109    def define(self, name: str, value: object, is_mut: bool = False):
110        self.vars[name] = value
111        self.mutable[name] = is_mut
def get(self, name: str) -> object: 
113    def get(self, name: str) -> object:
114        if name in self.vars:
115            return self.vars[name]
116        if self.parent:
117            return self.parent.get(name)
118        raise NameError(f"Undefined variable: '{name}'")
def set(self, name: str, value: object): 
120    def set(self, name: str, value: object):
121        if name in self.vars:
122            if not self.mutable.get(name, False):
123                raise TypeError(f"Cannot assign to immutable variable: '{name}'")
124            self.vars[name] = value
125            return
126        if self.parent:
127            self.parent.set(name, value)
128            return
129        raise NameError(f"Undefined variable: '{name}'")
def has(self, name: str) -> bool: 
131    def has(self, name: str) -> bool:
132        return name in self.vars or bool(self.parent and self.parent.has(name))
def enter_scope(self) -> Environment: 
134    def enter_scope(self) -> 'Environment':
135        return Environment(self)
@dataclass
class Diagnostic: 
109@dataclass
110class Diagnostic:
111    code: str
112    message: str
113    span: tuple  # (line_start, col_start, line_end, col_end)
114    severity: str = "error"  # "error", "warning", "info"
115
116    def format(self, source_lines: list[str] | None = None) -> str:
117        result = f"[{self.code}] {self.severity}: {self.message}"
118        if source_lines and len(source_lines) > 0:
119            line, col = self.span[0], self.span[1]
120            if 0 < line <= len(source_lines):
121                source_line = source_lines[line - 1].rstrip("\n")
122                result += f"\n  | {source_line}"
123                caret = " " * (col - 1) + "^" if col > 0 else "^"
124                result += f"\n  | {caret}"
125        return result
Diagnostic(code: str, message: str, span: tuple, severity: str = 'error')
code: str
message: str
span: tuple
severity: str = 'error'
def format(self, source_lines: list[str] | None = None) -> str: 
116    def format(self, source_lines: list[str] | None = None) -> str:
117        result = f"[{self.code}] {self.severity}: {self.message}"
118        if source_lines and len(source_lines) > 0:
119            line, col = self.span[0], self.span[1]
120            if 0 < line <= len(source_lines):
121                source_line = source_lines[line - 1].rstrip("\n")
122                result += f"\n  | {source_line}"
123                caret = " " * (col - 1) + "^" if col > 0 else "^"
124                result += f"\n  | {caret}"
125        return result
@dataclass
class DiagnosticBundle: 
128@dataclass
129class DiagnosticBundle:
130    diagnostics: list[Diagnostic] = field(default_factory=list)
131    source_lines: list[str] | None = None
132
133    def add(self, diagnostic: Diagnostic):
134        self.diagnostics.append(diagnostic)
135
136    def has_errors(self) -> bool:
137        return any(d.severity == "error" for d in self.diagnostics)
138
139    def format_all(self) -> str:
140        if not self.diagnostics:
141            return "No diagnostics."
142        parts = [f"Found {len(self.diagnostics)} diagnostic(s):"]
143        for d in self.diagnostics:
144            parts.append(d.format(self.source_lines))
145        return "\n\n".join(parts)
146
147    def __len__(self):
148        return len(self.diagnostics)
149
150    def __bool__(self):
151        return len(self.diagnostics) > 0
DiagnosticBundle( diagnostics: list[Diagnostic] = <factory>, source_lines: list[str] | None = None)
diagnostics: list[Diagnostic]
source_lines: list[str] | None = None
def add(self, diagnostic: Diagnostic): 
133    def add(self, diagnostic: Diagnostic):
134        self.diagnostics.append(diagnostic)
def has_errors(self) -> bool: 
136    def has_errors(self) -> bool:
137        return any(d.severity == "error" for d in self.diagnostics)
def format_all(self) -> str: 
139    def format_all(self) -> str:
140        if not self.diagnostics:
141            return "No diagnostics."
142        parts = [f"Found {len(self.diagnostics)} diagnostic(s):"]
143        for d in self.diagnostics:
144            parts.append(d.format(self.source_lines))
145        return "\n\n".join(parts)
class DiagnosticSeverity(enum.Enum): 
10class DiagnosticSeverity(Enum):
11    ERROR = "error"
12    WARNING = "warning"
13    INFO = "info"
14
15    def __str__(self):
16        return self.value
ERROR = <DiagnosticSeverity.ERROR: 'error'>
WARNING = <DiagnosticSeverity.WARNING: 'warning'>
INFO = <DiagnosticSeverity.INFO: 'info'>
ERROR_CODES = {'E001': "Unrecognized character '{char}'", 'E002': 'Unterminated string literal', 'E003': 'Unterminated block comment', 'E010': "Duplicate binding: '{name}' already defined in this scope", 'E011': "Unknown identifier: '{name}'", 'E012': "Cannot resolve module path: '{path}'", 'E020': "Cannot assign to immutable variable '{name}'", 'E021': 'Type mismatch: cannot assign {found} to variable of type {expected}', 'E022': "Type mismatch: operator '{op}' requires {expected} operands, got {found}", 'E023': 'Type mismatch: return type {found} does not match declared return type {expected}', 'E024': 'Type mismatch: condition must be Bool, got {found}', 'E030': 'Call arity mismatch: {name} expects {expected} arguments but got {got}', 'E040': "Module not found: '{module}'", 'E041': "Name '{name}' not exported by module '{module}'", 'E042': "Circular import detected for module '{module}'", 'E050': 'Governance violation: {detail}', 'E053': "Cannot call governed function '{name}' from core mode", 'E060': 'Reservoir is empty', 'E061': 'Index out of bounds: reservoir size is {size}, got index {index}', 'E062': 'Cannot flood onto non-reservoir type', 'E070': 'Quenched value is empty (parched)', 'E071': 'Cannot condense on non-quenched type', 'E072': 'Cannot evaporate on non-quenched type', 'E073': 'Quenched type mismatch: expected {expected}, got {found}', 'E080': 'Pipe type mismatch: left side produces {left_type}, right side expects {right_type}', 'E090': 'Cascade call failed: {detail}', 'E091': 'Await used outside cascade context', 'E092': 'Cannot cascade non-function type', 'E093': 'Cascade timeout after {timeout}ms', 'E100': 'Internal error: {detail}', 'E200': 'TARL policy evaluation error: {detail}', 'E201': 'Unknown policy rule: {rule}', 'E202': 'Policy expression error: {detail}', 'E300': 'Shadow Thirst mutation validation error: {detail}', 'E301': 'Shadow plane isolation violation: {detail}', 'E302': 'Determinism violation: {detail}', 'E303': 'Resource estimate exceeded: {detail}', 'E304': 'Purity violation in invariant block: {detail}', 'E305': 'Memory evaporation estimate exceeded: {detail}', 'E306': 'Canonical convergence failure: {detail}', 'E307': 'Promotion blocked by critical failure: {detail}', 'E400': 'TSCG parse error: {detail}', 'E401': 'Unknown TSCG symbol: {symbol}', 'E500': 'TSCG-B frame error: {detail}', 'E501': 'CRC32 checksum mismatch', 'E502': 'SHA-256 hash mismatch', 'E503': 'Invalid frame magic: got {magic}', 'E900': 'IO error: {detail}', 'E901': 'Syntax error: {detail}'}
def format_diagnostic( error: Diagnostic, source_lines: list[str] | None = None) -> str: 
154def format_diagnostic(error: Diagnostic, source_lines: list[str] | None = None) -> str:
155    """Convenience function to format a single diagnostic with source context."""
156    return error.format(source_lines)

Convenience function to format a single diagnostic with source context.

def resolve_import(path_str: str, locked: bool = False) -> object: 
676def resolve_import(path_str: str, locked: bool = False) -> object:
677    """
678    Resolve an import path string to a module object.
679    Handles 'thirst::module' syntax for stdlib modules.
680    When locked=True, verifies the dependency exists in thirsty.lock
681    before resolving.
682    """
683    # When locked, load and verify against lockfile
684    if locked:
685        lock = load_lockfile(".")
686        if not lock or "dependencies" not in lock or not lock["dependencies"]:
687            raise ImportError(
688                "Lockfile check failed: thirsty.lock not found or empty. "
689                "Run 'thirsty lock' first to generate it."
690            )
691        # Extract dependency name from path_str (e.g. "thirst::crypto" -> "crypto")
692        dep_name = path_str
693        if path_str.startswith("thirst::"):
694            dep_name = path_str[len("thirst::"):]
695        if not check_lock_integrity(dep_name, "*", lock):
696            raise ImportError(
697                f"Lockfile integrity check failed: '{dep_name}' not found in thirsty.lock. "
698                f"Run 'thirsty add {dep_name}' to add it."
699            )
700
701    # Check cache first
702    if path_str in ModuleCache:
703        return ModuleCache[path_str]
704
705    # Check stdlib modules
706    if path_str in STDLIB_MODULES:
707        module = STDLIB_MODULES[path_str]()
708        ModuleCache[path_str] = module
709        return module
710
711    # A 'thirst::' path not matched in STDLIB_MODULES above is an unknown module.
712    if path_str.startswith("thirst::"):
713        raise ImportError(f"Module not found: {path_str}")
714
715    # Try to load from a .thirsty source file: parse, interpret, and expose its
716    # top-level bindings (functions and `drink` values) as a module dict — the
717    # same shape stdlib modules use, so `import "x.thirsty" as m; m.fn(...)`
718    # works through the interpreter's dict member access.
719    if path_str.endswith(".thirsty"):
720        if not os.path.exists(path_str):
721            raise ImportError(f"File not found: {path_str}")
722        # Lazy imports: the interpreter imports this module at top level.
723        from utf.thirsty_lang.interpreter import Interpreter
724        from utf.thirsty_lang.lexer import Lexer
725        from utf.thirsty_lang.parser import Parser
726
727        with open(path_str) as f:
728            source = f.read()
729        parser = Parser(Lexer(source).lex())
730        ast = parser.parse()
731        if parser.errors:
732            raise ImportError(f"Failed to import '{path_str}': {parser.errors[0]}")
733        interp = Interpreter()
734        baseline = set(interp.env.vars)  # builtins to exclude from the module
735        interp.interpret(ast)
736        module = {
737            name: value
738            for name, value in interp.env.vars.items()
739            if name not in baseline
740        }
741        ModuleCache[path_str] = module
742        return module
743
744    raise ImportError(f"Module not found: {path_str}")

Resolve an import path string to a module object. Handles 'thirst::module' syntax for stdlib modules. When locked=True, verifies the dependency exists in thirsty.lock before resolving.

def get_builtin(name: str) -> Callable[..., typing.Any]: 
747def get_builtin(name: str) -> Callable[..., Any]:
748    """Get a builtin function by name."""
749    if name in BUILTINS:
750        return BUILTINS[name]
751    raise KeyError(f"Builtin not found: {name}")

Get a builtin function by name.

def list_stdlib_modules() -> list[str]: 
754def list_stdlib_modules() -> list[str]:
755    """List all available stdlib module paths."""
756    return sorted(STDLIB_MODULES.keys())

List all available stdlib module paths.

def list_builtins() -> list[str]: 
759def list_builtins() -> list[str]:
760    """List all builtin function names."""
761    return sorted(BUILTINS.keys())

List all builtin function names.

def format(ast: Program) -> str: 
405def format(ast: Program) -> str:
406    """Top-level format function. Returns formatted source code string."""
407    return format_program(ast) + "\n"

Top-level format function. Returns formatted source code string.

class PackageManager: 
 13class PackageManager:
 14    """Manage Thirsty-Lang packages (dependencies, manifest, lockfile)."""
 15
 16    def __init__(self, project_root: str = "."):
 17        self.project_root = project_root
 18        self.manifest_path = os.path.join(project_root, "thirsty.toml")
 19        self.lock_path = os.path.join(project_root, "thirsty.lock")
 20        self.manifest: dict = {}
 21        self.lock: dict = {}
 22
 23    def parse_manifest(self, path: str | None = None) -> dict:
 24        """Parse a thirsty.toml manifest file."""
 25        if path:
 26            self.manifest_path = path
 27        if not os.path.exists(self.manifest_path):
 28            return {}
 29        with open(self.manifest_path) as f:
 30            content = f.read()
 31        self.manifest = self._parse_toml(content)
 32        return self.manifest
 33
 34    def _parse_toml(self, content: str) -> dict:
 35        """Simple TOML parser for thirsty.toml format."""
 36        result: dict[str, Any] = {}
 37        current_section = result
 38
 39        for line in content.split("\n"):
 40            line = line.strip()
 41            if not line or line.startswith("#"):
 42                continue
 43
 44            # Section header: [section]
 45            section_match = re.match(r'^\[([^\]]+)\]$', line)
 46            if section_match:
 47                section_name = section_match.group(1)
 48                parts = section_name.split(".")
 49                current_section = result
 50                for part in parts:
 51                    if part not in current_section:
 52                        current_section[part] = {}
 53                    current_section = current_section[part]
 54                continue
 55
 56            # Key-value: key = value
 57            kv_match = re.match(r'^([^=]+)=(.+)$', line)
 58            if kv_match:
 59                key = kv_match.group(1).strip()
 60                raw_value = kv_match.group(2).strip()
 61                value = self._parse_toml_value(raw_value)
 62                current_section[key] = value
 63
 64        return result
 65
 66    def _parse_toml_value(self, raw: str) -> object:
 67        """Parse a TOML value."""
 68        if raw.startswith('"') and raw.endswith('"'):
 69            return raw[1:-1]
 70        if raw.startswith("'") and raw.endswith("'"):
 71            return raw[1:-1]
 72        if raw == "true":
 73            return True
 74        if raw == "false":
 75            return False
 76        if raw == "none" or raw == "null":
 77            return None
 78        # Array: [1, 2, 3]
 79        if raw.startswith("[") and raw.endswith("]"):
 80            inner = raw[1:-1].strip()
 81            if not inner:
 82                return []
 83            items = []
 84            for item in re.split(r',\s*', inner):
 85                items.append(self._parse_toml_value(item.strip()))
 86            return items
 87        # Inline table: {key = value}
 88        if raw.startswith("{") and raw.endswith("}"):
 89            inner = raw[1:-1].strip()
 90            result = {}
 91            for pair in re.split(r',\s*', inner):
 92                if "=" in pair:
 93                    k, v = pair.split("=", 1)
 94                    result[k.strip()] = self._parse_toml_value(v.strip())
 95            return result
 96        # Number
 97        try:
 98            if "." in raw:
 99                return float(raw)
100            return int(raw)
101        except ValueError:
102            pass
103        return raw
104
105    def parse_lock(self, path: str | None = None) -> dict:
106        """Parse thirsty.lock lockfile."""
107        if path:
108            self.lock_path = path
109        if not os.path.exists(self.lock_path):
110            return {}
111        with open(self.lock_path) as f:
112            content = f.read()
113        self.lock = json.loads(content) if content.strip() else {}
114        return self.lock
115
116    def generate_lock(self, dependencies: dict | None = None) -> dict:
117        """Generate a lockfile with SHA-256 hashes."""
118        if dependencies is None:
119            dependencies = self.manifest.get("dependencies", {})
120
121        lock_entries = {}
122        for name, version_spec in dependencies.items():
123            version = version_spec if isinstance(version_spec, str) else str(version_spec)
124            entry = {
125                "version": version,
126                "resolved": f"thirsty-registry://{name}@{version}",
127                "integrity": self._compute_integrity(name, version),
128            }
129            lock_entries[name] = entry
130
131        self.lock = {
132            "lockfile_version": 1,
133            "dependencies": lock_entries,
134        }
135        return self.lock
136
137    def _compute_integrity(self, name: str, version: str) -> str:
138        """Compute SHA-256 integrity hash for a dependency entry."""
139        content = f"{name}@{version}"
140        return "sha256-" + hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest()
141
142    def write_lock(self, path: str | None = None) -> bool:
143        """Write the lockfile."""
144        if path:
145            self.lock_path = path
146        try:
147            with open(self.lock_path, 'w') as f:
148                json.dump(self.lock, f, indent=2)
149            return True
150        except Exception:
151            return False
152
153    def verify_integrity(self) -> list[dict]:
154        """Verify all lockfile entries match their computed hashes."""
155        violations = []
156        for name, entry in self.lock.get("dependencies", {}).items():
157            expected = entry.get("integrity", "")
158            computed = self._compute_integrity(name, entry.get("version", ""))
159            if expected != computed:
160                violations.append({
161                    "name": name,
162                    "expected": expected,
163                    "computed": computed,
164                })
165        return violations
166
167    def add_dependency(self, name: str, version_spec: str = "*") -> bool:
168        """Add a dependency to the manifest."""
169        deps = self.manifest.setdefault("dependencies", {})
170        deps[name] = version_spec
171        return self._write_manifest()
172
173    def remove_dependency(self, name: str) -> bool:
174        """Remove a dependency from the manifest."""
175        deps = self.manifest.get("dependencies", {})
176        if name in deps:
177            del deps[name]
178            return self._write_manifest()
179        return False
180
181    def _write_manifest(self) -> bool:
182        """Write the manifest back to disk."""
183        try:
184            content = self._format_toml(self.manifest)
185            with open(self.manifest_path, 'w') as f:
186                f.write(content)
187            return True
188        except Exception:
189            return False
190
191    def _format_toml(self, data: dict, prefix: str = "") -> str:
192        """Format a dict as a TOML string."""
193        lines = []
194        for key, value in data.items():
195            if isinstance(value, dict) and key != "dependencies":
196                lines.append(f"\n[{prefix}{key}]")
197                for k2, v2 in value.items():
198                    lines.append(f"{k2} = {json.dumps(v2) if isinstance(v2, str) else v2}")
199            elif key == "dependencies" and isinstance(value, dict):
200                lines.append("\n[dependencies]")
201                for dep_name, dep_ver in value.items():
202                    lines.append(f"{dep_name} = {json.dumps(str(dep_ver))}")
203            else:
204                lines.append(f"{key} = {json.dumps(value) if isinstance(value, str) else value}")
205        return "\n".join(lines)
206
207    def audit_dependencies(self) -> list[dict]:
208        """Audit all dependencies for integrity and version issues."""
209        issues = []
210        # Parse manifest
211        self.parse_manifest()
212        # Parse lockfile
213        self.parse_lock()
214
215        manifest_deps = self.manifest.get("dependencies", {})
216        lock_deps = self.lock.get("dependencies", {})
217
218        for name in manifest_deps:
219            if name not in lock_deps:
220                issues.append({
221                    "type": "missing_lock",
222                    "name": name,
223                    "message": f"Dependency '{name}' is in manifest but not in lockfile"
224                })
225            else:
226                # Verify integrity
227                expected = lock_deps[name].get("integrity", "")
228                computed = self._compute_integrity(name, lock_deps[name].get("version", ""))
229                if expected != computed:
230                    issues.append({
231                        "type": "integrity_mismatch",
232                        "name": name,
233                        "message": f"Integrity mismatch for '{name}'"
234                    })
235
236        for name in lock_deps:
237            if name not in manifest_deps:
238                issues.append({
239                    "type": "orphan_lock",
240                    "name": name,
241                    "message": f"Dependency '{name}' is in lockfile but not in manifest"
242                })
243
244        return issues

Manage Thirsty-Lang packages (dependencies, manifest, lockfile).

PackageManager(project_root: str = '.') 
16    def __init__(self, project_root: str = "."):
17        self.project_root = project_root
18        self.manifest_path = os.path.join(project_root, "thirsty.toml")
19        self.lock_path = os.path.join(project_root, "thirsty.lock")
20        self.manifest: dict = {}
21        self.lock: dict = {}
project_root
manifest_path
lock_path
manifest: dict
lock: dict
def parse_manifest(self, path: str | None = None) -> dict: 
23    def parse_manifest(self, path: str | None = None) -> dict:
24        """Parse a thirsty.toml manifest file."""
25        if path:
26            self.manifest_path = path
27        if not os.path.exists(self.manifest_path):
28            return {}
29        with open(self.manifest_path) as f:
30            content = f.read()
31        self.manifest = self._parse_toml(content)
32        return self.manifest

Parse a thirsty.toml manifest file.

def parse_lock(self, path: str | None = None) -> dict: 
105    def parse_lock(self, path: str | None = None) -> dict:
106        """Parse thirsty.lock lockfile."""
107        if path:
108            self.lock_path = path
109        if not os.path.exists(self.lock_path):
110            return {}
111        with open(self.lock_path) as f:
112            content = f.read()
113        self.lock = json.loads(content) if content.strip() else {}
114        return self.lock

Parse thirsty.lock lockfile.

def generate_lock(self, dependencies: dict | None = None) -> dict: 
116    def generate_lock(self, dependencies: dict | None = None) -> dict:
117        """Generate a lockfile with SHA-256 hashes."""
118        if dependencies is None:
119            dependencies = self.manifest.get("dependencies", {})
120
121        lock_entries = {}
122        for name, version_spec in dependencies.items():
123            version = version_spec if isinstance(version_spec, str) else str(version_spec)
124            entry = {
125                "version": version,
126                "resolved": f"thirsty-registry://{name}@{version}",
127                "integrity": self._compute_integrity(name, version),
128            }
129            lock_entries[name] = entry
130
131        self.lock = {
132            "lockfile_version": 1,
133            "dependencies": lock_entries,
134        }
135        return self.lock

Generate a lockfile with SHA-256 hashes.

def write_lock(self, path: str | None = None) -> bool: 
142    def write_lock(self, path: str | None = None) -> bool:
143        """Write the lockfile."""
144        if path:
145            self.lock_path = path
146        try:
147            with open(self.lock_path, 'w') as f:
148                json.dump(self.lock, f, indent=2)
149            return True
150        except Exception:
151            return False

Write the lockfile.

def verify_integrity(self) -> list[dict]: 
153    def verify_integrity(self) -> list[dict]:
154        """Verify all lockfile entries match their computed hashes."""
155        violations = []
156        for name, entry in self.lock.get("dependencies", {}).items():
157            expected = entry.get("integrity", "")
158            computed = self._compute_integrity(name, entry.get("version", ""))
159            if expected != computed:
160                violations.append({
161                    "name": name,
162                    "expected": expected,
163                    "computed": computed,
164                })
165        return violations

Verify all lockfile entries match their computed hashes.

def add_dependency(self, name: str, version_spec: str = '*') -> bool: 
167    def add_dependency(self, name: str, version_spec: str = "*") -> bool:
168        """Add a dependency to the manifest."""
169        deps = self.manifest.setdefault("dependencies", {})
170        deps[name] = version_spec
171        return self._write_manifest()

Add a dependency to the manifest.

def remove_dependency(self, name: str) -> bool: 
173    def remove_dependency(self, name: str) -> bool:
174        """Remove a dependency from the manifest."""
175        deps = self.manifest.get("dependencies", {})
176        if name in deps:
177            del deps[name]
178            return self._write_manifest()
179        return False

Remove a dependency from the manifest.

def audit_dependencies(self) -> list[dict]: 
207    def audit_dependencies(self) -> list[dict]:
208        """Audit all dependencies for integrity and version issues."""
209        issues = []
210        # Parse manifest
211        self.parse_manifest()
212        # Parse lockfile
213        self.parse_lock()
214
215        manifest_deps = self.manifest.get("dependencies", {})
216        lock_deps = self.lock.get("dependencies", {})
217
218        for name in manifest_deps:
219            if name not in lock_deps:
220                issues.append({
221                    "type": "missing_lock",
222                    "name": name,
223                    "message": f"Dependency '{name}' is in manifest but not in lockfile"
224                })
225            else:
226                # Verify integrity
227                expected = lock_deps[name].get("integrity", "")
228                computed = self._compute_integrity(name, lock_deps[name].get("version", ""))
229                if expected != computed:
230                    issues.append({
231                        "type": "integrity_mismatch",
232                        "name": name,
233                        "message": f"Integrity mismatch for '{name}'"
234                    })
235
236        for name in lock_deps:
237            if name not in manifest_deps:
238                issues.append({
239                    "type": "orphan_lock",
240                    "name": name,
241                    "message": f"Dependency '{name}' is in lockfile but not in manifest"
242                })
243
244        return issues

Audit all dependencies for integrity and version issues.

def main(): 
107def main():
108    from utf.thirsty_lang.cli import main as _cli_main
109    return _cli_main()
